A clockwork mage is a lethal blend of magic and machinery. Each of these clockworks is imbued with an arcane stone at its core that powers spells through the wand embedded in its chest. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Clockwork Mage](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1097) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 50 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +17 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -5 **Wind-Up** 24 hours, DC 26, standby **Items** _clockwork wand_ --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 115 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** electricity 10, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 10 **Clockwork Wand** The clockwork mage uses a mechanical wand as a focus to channel magical energy. This wand is built into the clockwork mage's chest, with only the crystal at the end exposed. The mage can Interact to the remove the wand, or someone else can remove it with a DC 31 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69). The clockwork mage becomes unable to cast any spells except cantrips while the wand is removed.
When removed, the _clockwork wand_ is a magic wand containing the last 2nd-level innate spell the clockwork mage cast (the GM determines the spell randomly if it has not cast any eligible spells). The spells are placed within the wand while the mage is created, and the creator can substitute other arcane spells of the appropriate level. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d10+6 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **1st** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111), [Floating Disk](/Spells.aspx?ID=124), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) - **2nd** [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309) - **4th** [Blink](/Spells.aspx?ID=27), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) - **5th** [Black Tentacles](/Spells.aspx?ID=23), [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) **Energize Clockwork Wand** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The clockwork mage regains a spell it has already cast that day. It must spend 1 hour of its operational time, or 2 hours if the spell is 3rd level or higher.
name: Clockwork Mage

markdown:
Clockwork dragons are a marvel of clockwork design. These powerful masterpieces have the ability to fly, making them versatile and dangerous killers. A clockwork dragon's winding mechanism is more efficient than those of other clockworks, allowing it to partially reuse energy generated by flapping its wings. Creating a clockwork dragon requires not only an advanced understanding of clockwork design, but also a greater cache of rare resources, as its body requires solid reinforcement. As a result, most clockwork dragons have adamantine fortification. Creating a clockwork dragon without adamantine is entirely possible, though such dragons are typically smaller and more fragile. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Clockwork Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1098) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 51 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33 **Str** +9 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** -5 **Wind-Up** 1 week, DC 35, standby --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +28 **Will** +25 **HP** 265 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** electricity 15, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 15 **Self-Destruct** A clockwork dragon must use this reaction unless specifically programmed otherwise by its creator; **Trigger** The clockwork dragon is reduced to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The dragon screeches to a stop and emits a steady, loud ticking sound. At the beginning of what would have been its next turn, the dragon explodes, dealing 12d10 piercing damage in a 40-foot emanation (DC 37 basic Reflex save).
An adjacent creature can cancel the self-destruct sequence by succeeding at a DC 37 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69). --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** adamantine jaws +33 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+17 piercing **Melee** adamantine claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing **Melee** tail +31 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning **Melee** wing +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+15 piercing **Breath Weapon** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) **Effect** The clockwork dragon breathes a spray of flaming oil that deals 16d6 fire damage in a 40-foot cone (DC 37 basic Reflex save). Creatures that fail their saves are covered in burning oil and take 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The clockwork dragon can't use Breath Weapon again for 2 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The clockwork dragon makes two claw Strikes and one wing Strike in any order. **Spearing Tail** The clockwork dragon attacks with the sharp point of its tail. It makes a tail Strike against each creature in a 20-foot line, rolling the attack roll once and applying the result to each target. Any creature hit takes 4d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) (doubled on a critical hit). This counts as two attacks for the dragon's multiple attack penalty.
name: Clockwork Dragon

Busy road builders might unknowingly use cobble mites in construction. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 16 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11 [Cobbleswarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1099) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 52, [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 83 **Perception** +9; no vision, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (precise) 40 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Clutching Cobbles** The cobbleswarm's space is difficult terrain.
**Tremorsense** A cobbleswarm's tremorsense is a precise sense out to 40 feet and an imprecise sense out to 80 feet. A cobbleswarm can't sense anything beyond the range of its tremorsense. --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +10 **Will** +7 **HP** 20 **Immunities** precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=36), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) **Resistances** piercing 5, slashing 5 **Weaknesses** area damage 3, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 3 --- **Speed** 20 feet, burrow 10 feet **Grasping Bites** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) The cobbleswarm attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check and compares the result to the Fortitude DC of each creature in its space. This counts as two attacks for the cobbleswarm's multiple attack penalty.
**Critical Success** The creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31), takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage, and is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the cobbleswarm until the end of the cobbleswarm's next turn.
**Success** The creature falls prone. **Pummeling Assault** Each foe in the cobbleswarm's space takes 2d4 bludgeoning damage (DC 17 basic Reflex save).
Cobble Mite Pets Characters looking to keep a cobble mite as a pet can usually buy one in a large city for 1 gp. Cobble mites are content as pets if they're pampered and well-fed. Searching for one in the wild takes careful hunting and risks finding cobble mites laired together in a cobbleswarm. A character with a familiar can choose a cobble mite, which must have the burrower familiar ability.
name: Cobbleswarm

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Consonite Choir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1101)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Floods of crystalline blades called consonite choirs surge through the caverns and tunnels of the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5). Although their resonant chimes can be among the most beautiful music in the planes, those who intrude on their territory are instead confronted with a bone-shaking wall of sound and a barrage of razor-sharp crystals.

 The blades form naturally, just one of the many wondrous types of crystal on the Plane of Earth. Razor-edged consonite stalactites hum and chime along with the plane's grinding movement. As large collections of these crystals begin to communicate with each other through their shared song, they awaken into a sort of musical collective.

 Eventually, consonite choirs' songs grow loud and complex enough to shake these colonies loose of their stone moorings and free them to explore the Plane of Earth. Consonite choirs typically prefer to stay near their spawning grounds but might be coerced to perform in the homes of wealthy [shaitans](/Monsters.aspx?ID=213) or serve other powerful creatures of earth. They willingly leave the comfort of their homes to hunt down the hated [xorns](/Monsters.aspx?ID=184), who enjoy the flavor of their immobile consonite brethren.

 Consonite choirs who leave the Plane of Earth, either through natural portals or by answering the calls of powerful rituals, often find the open spaces of other planes more to their liking. They enjoy the sensation of high winds moving through their chimes and search tirelessly for skilled performance partners. Crueler fates trap other choirs—more powerful beings might mystically bind them to service or bully them into becoming living trophies.

 Over years of movement, the individual crystal blades of a choir naturally chip and break down. In their native caverns, consonite choirs can easily find new members. Trapped elsewhere, they must rely on others for replacement blades or eventually fall into torpor.

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28
</column>

</row>

**Resonant Chimes** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) 30 feet. The consonite choir's tones resonate within physical objects. A creature that begins its turn in the aura deals an additional 2d6 sonic damage on any Strike using a weapon.

**Damage** 6d6 piercing plus 2d6 sonic

**Swarming Chimes** <actions string="Single Action" />  The swarm deals 4d6 slashing damage plus 2d6 sonic damage to each enemy in the swarm's space (DC 33 basic Reflex save). On a critical failure, the enemy is also [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=8) for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Consonite Blades</title>

Consonite crystals form sharp, if fragile, blades. A weapon made from a single perfect crystal of consonite naturally forms a [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306) weapon rune, but it can't have other non-fundamental runes etched into it. Damaged or incomplete crystals are still valuable for creating musical instruments or alchemical items like [thunderstones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=79).
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Coral Capuchin](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1102)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A coral capuchin resembles a hairless pink monkey with large eyes and batlike wings. Something of a tropical nuisance, these amphibious creatures follow trade ships, hoping to filch small valuables they can take home or perhaps find a sailor they can befriend to secure a regular supply of food.

 Coral capuchins live in tropical climates, and while they can breathe water or air with equal ease, their membranous skin means they rely heavily on having a nearby source of water. Fresh- and saltwater serve equally well for this purpose, though the creatures generally congregate near oceans and seas for other reasons. Jungles, islands, and warm coastlines are their most common nesting sites, though they usually hide their nests high in trees or in other locations out of easy reach of predators, scavengers, or anything that might steal the trinkets they've collected.

 These little flyers are insatiably curious, and they love to follow humanoids around. Capuchins largely agree that humanoids have the best food and trinkets. Smarter than animals and capable of rudimentary speech, the creatures can be quite useful with the right training, learning how seek out sunken treasures or hunt down rats and other pests on a ship. Some spellcasters residing along tropical coastlines have taken coral capuchins as familiars, though many claim the critters as far more trouble than any benefits they provide are worth. A capuchin familiar might pilfer spellcasting components, gemstones, vials, crafting materials, and other trinkets from their master, though a clever magic user will intersperse more appealing (and less valuable) baubles to draw their familiar's attention away from actually important items.

 Capuchin clans live together under a single matriarch, a slightly larger female who can be identified by her unusually prismatic skin and colorful eyes. These females refuse to be tamed or kept as pets and violently resist all forms of capture. The most powerful among them even possess divine powers which they call upon to protect their homes and kin. In return, the remainder of the clan fights fiercely to protect their matriarch, defending her (and her trinkets) from threats far larger than themselves.

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

**Skills**

<row gap="medium">

**Moisture Dependency** A coral capuchin must stay in or near water, or else it begins drying out. It can remain away from water for only 12 hours before its membranous skin starts to turn brittle and flake, taking 1d6 damage per hour until it is doused in water.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet, fly 15 feet, swim 15 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d8 piercing plus capuchin's curse

**Capuchin's Curse**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) If a capuchin damages a creature with its jaws Strike, the creature gains both a blessing and a curse that last for 1d6 hours. While the blessing doubles the amount of time that the affected creature can hold its breath, the curse imposes a quickened version of the capuchin's moisture dependency, taking 1d6 damage every 10 minutes they go without water. A cursed creature can fully immerse itself in water as a 3-action activity to attempt a DC 17 Fortitude save, ending both the blessing and the curse on a success.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Coral Capuchin Nests</title>

The largest known coral capuchin nests are found around the Shackles and coastlines of the Mwangi Expanse. Preferring saltwater oceans over freshwater rivers or inlets for hunting, coral capuchins stake out territories within reach of shipping lanes and places frequented by humanoids. A nest's treasure hoard usually includes small valuables, such as gems, coins, and jewelry—anything waterproof they can easily abscond with.
</aside>
name: Coral Capuchin

markdown:
Creatures that once held particular items dear can be bound to them after death, their souls inhabiting the item from within. These might be the faithful haunting symbols of their faith, warriors clinging to their old weapons, or the greedy inhabiting their precious wealth. These spirits' physical bodies have long since degraded, but they keep their corrupted relics pristine. The spirits manifest on the unsuspecting or curious, determined to destroy anyone who would dare show any interest in their one precious possession. Though rare, there are several kinds of corrupted relics. The bound spirit can manifest in different ways, from animating an ancient weapon to possessing anyone who dons the item. Destroying the relic often ends up being the simplest, most direct way to banish the haunt itself. Destroying or dispelling the spirit without destroying the relic, however, can sometimes leave priceless treasure behind. The amulet shown here has the [air](/Relics.aspx?Aspect=1) and [death](/Relics.aspx?Aspect=4) aspects. Its minor gifts, [deadly spark](/Relics.aspx?ID=1) and [death gaze](/Relics.aspx?ID=19), power some of the spirit's abilities. Someone who claims the relic might gain different gifts from those aspects. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 24 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Corrupted Relic](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1103) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 55 **Perception** +9; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** -5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Items** amulet --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +11 **Will** +11 **HP** 50 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Amulet Relic** ([curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The corrupted relic is an amulet cursed to contain a malevolent spirit. The statistics in this stat block are for the spirit when it's manifested (see Manifest). The amulet can't take any actions except Sudden Manifestation and Manifest. As long as the spirit is manifested, the amulet is immune to spells and magical abilities and uses the saves listed here.
Regardless of whether the spirit is manifested, the amulet can be targeted by Strikes (AC 21, Hardness 5, BT 10). It is not [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222). If the amulet becomes broken, the spirit is bound within and can't Manifest, and if the amulet is destroyed, so is the spirit. Destroying the spirit frees the amulet from its curse.
**Sudden Manifestation** **Trigger** The relic detects a creature using its lifesense; **Effect** The relic Manifests. If this causes an encounter to begin, the relic gains a +4 status bonus to its initiative roll. --- **Speed** fly 30 feet **Melee** spectral hand +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d6+3 negative **Ranged** deadly spark +14 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [range 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d12 electricity **Death Gaze** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) The spirit targets a creature it can see within 30 feet, dealing to it 4d6 negative damage (DC 20 basic Fortitude save). A creature with negative healing instead regains 4d6 HP and is then temporarily immune for 10 minutes. **Manifest** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) **Requirements** The relic detects at least one creature using its lifesense; **Effect** The spirit within the relic manifests in an open space within 10 feet of the amulet. When it does, the relic teleports around its neck. The spirit remains in its manifested state until it no longer senses any living creatures within range of its lifesense.
Haunted Relics Relics are a special type of magic item that can grow in power as their wielders unlock additional abilities. The full rules for relics and their aspects can be found [here](/Rules.aspx?ID=1096).
name: Corrupted Relic

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mix Couatl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1104)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Mix couatls (pronounced “meesh”) are guides to fledgling societies, providing fundamental information such as farming techniques, medicinal expertise, or more esoteric knowledge like the arcane arts.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Couatl_MixCouatl.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Mix Couatl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1104)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
<trait label="Couatl" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=298" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 56

**Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +21, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** +4

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +4
</row>

**Star Child** The mix couatl is difficult to discern against starry skies. The mix couatl can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) in the air at night without cover or being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +14 

**Ref** +15 

**Will** +19 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 135

</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d10+9 piercing plus mix couatl venom and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 26, attack +18
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171), [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307)
- **1st**
[Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53), [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=189)
- **2nd**
[Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (self only), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293)
- **3rd**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (at will)
- **4th**
[Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=294)
- **7th**
[Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only)

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  2d10+4 bludgeoning, DC 26

**Gift of Knowledge**   When a mix couatl casts [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) on a willing creature, the mix couatl can Sustain the Spell to modify memories for up to 60 continuous minutes. A mix couatl can grant knowledge of a particular skill to the target as part of the spell. The mix couatl chooses Engineering [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), Farming Lore, Fishing Lore, Hunting Lore, or Mercantile Lore. The target becomes permanently trained in the chosen skill. A creature can benefit from Gift of Knowledge only once.

**Mix Couatl Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) A mix couatl's venom deals good damage rather than poison damage to [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70); **Saving Throw** DC 26 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage, [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), and stupefied 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d8 poison damage, stunned 1, and stupefied 2 (1 round)

**Wrap in Coils** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The mix couatl has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) in their jaws; **Effect** The mix couatl moves the creature into their coils, freeing their jaws to make attacks, then uses Constrict against the creature. The mix couatl can hold as many creatures in their coils as will fit in their space.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-128" />
name: Mix Couatl

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Xiuh Couatl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1105)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Fiercer than other couatls, xiuh couatls (pronounced “shoo”) are dedicated to seeking out malevolent creatures and offering a final opportunity at redemption.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 35

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Couatl_XiuhCouatl.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 12">[Xiuh Couatl](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1105)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="NG" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Beast" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=20" />
<trait label="Couatl" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=298" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 57

**Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), thoughtsense (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +26, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +23, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +3

**Con** +4

**Int** +5

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Thoughtsense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The xiuh couatl senses all non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) creatures at the listed range.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 33 

**Fort** +20 

**Ref** +19 

**Will** +25 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 220

</row>

**Immunities**
electricity, fire

**Weaknesses**
cold 8

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, fly 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d10+10 piercing plus 2d8 fire, xiuh couatl venom, and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 32, attack +24
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (self only; at will)
- **6th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=39), [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=40), [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=90), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194) (at will), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=195), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271), [Sending](/Spells.aspx?ID=272), [Zone of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=379)
- **7th**
[Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) (self only)

**Rituals** DC 32

- **4th**
[Atone](/Rituals.aspx?ID=2)

**Breath Weapon** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The xiuh couatl breaths a gout of flame and lightning in an 80-foot line that deals 5d8 electricity damage and 5d8 fire damage (DC 32 basic Reflex save). The xiuh couatl can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds.

**[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d10+10 bludgeoning plus 1d8 electricity, DC 32

**Instrument of Retribution** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The xiuh couatl transforms into any simple or martial weapon. The weapon is always a _+2 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303) weapon_ and can be of any size, chosen by the xiuh couatl when they transform. In weapon form, the xiuh couatl gains Hardness equal to that of the weapon into which they transform, and they retain their Hit Points, saving throws, senses, telepathy, and traits while in weapon form. The xiuh couatl can revert to their normal form by spending a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

**Wrap in Coils** <actions string="Single Action" />  **Requirements** The xiuh couatl has a Medium or smaller creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) in their jaws; **Effect** The xiuh couatl moves the creature into their coils, freeing their jaws to make attacks, then uses Constrict against the creature. The xiuh couatl can hold as many creatures in their coils as will fit in their space.

**Xiuh Couatl Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) A xiuh couatl's poison deals good damage rather than poison damage to [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70); **Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d8 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d10 poison damage, enfeebled 1, and –5-foot status penalty to all Speeds (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d8 poison damage, enfeebled 2, and –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds (1 round)

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-128" />
name: Xiuh Couatl

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hermit Crab Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1106)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

When hermit crabs find a shell that is too large, they lie in wait for others to arrive and move into the shell, abandoning a possible replacement. This can form a large chain of queuing crabs waiting to upgrade their shells. Interruptions can aggravate the crabs, resulting in attacks against the unfortunate source of disruption.

<column gap="tiny">

([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

</column>

</column>

</column>

</row>

</column>

---

**Swarming Snips** <actions string="Single Action" />  Each enemy in the swarm's space takes 2d8 piercing damage (DC 20 basic Reflex save). Creatures that fail this save also take 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Hermit Crab](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1107)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Lucky hermit crabs with plentiful resources can live for decades and grow to tremendous sizes, often with magical help. Too large for most natural shells, giant hermit crabs use a variety of debris instead, including wagons, boats, treasure chests, and even abandoned pieces of giant armor.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

markdown:
When a village is threatened by a horde of bandits but there's no one to help, when a monastery's divine relic must be guarded but the last monk is on their deathbed, when the world can be saved only through the use of an artifact no one alive remembers, nature responds by creating a crossroads guardian, a naturally occurring entity of primal magic. Every crossroads guardian exists to satisfy a particular need—usually a quest that must be carried out, helpless individuals who must be saved from harm, or an object or location which must be guarded. Although they often appear at actual crossroads, a crossroads guardian can materialize anywhere there is a community, from a giant metropolis to a [lizardfolk](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=15) village in a remote swamp. Some crossroads guardians are martial beings—defending a mystic fountain against all intruders, for example—but most are helpers and assistants, guiding heroes through the wilderness to where they're needed. A guardian is a skilled warrior and can defend a location, but when its purpose involves a quest, or the need is so great that the guardian isn't up to the task alone, the guardian must persuade others to voluntarily accept their quest. Once a crossroads guardian has fulfilled its purpose, it either shifts to satisfy a new need or quest—often changing its alignment and abilities as well—or else slowly vanishes, returning to the raw primal magic from which it was formed. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Crossroads Guardian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1108) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 59 **Perception** +19; recognize hero **Languages** [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +16, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +16, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +16, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +17, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 **Bound** A crossroads guardian is bound to the site of its creation, but it can leave so long as it remains within 100 feet of the object it is guarding or individuals under a [geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) to fulfill the guardian's need. If it strays farther than 100 feet, it vanishes and reappears within 1 day at the site of its creation.
**Recognize Hero** A crossroads guardian knows the name, lineage, and significant history of everyone who speaks to it. If that individual has two or more identities, the crossroads guardian knows all those identities and which one is most true. **Items** [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9), [Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=24) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +13 **Will** +19 **HP** 115 (primal purpose) **Primal Purpose** A crossroads guardian can't be permanently destroyed while its need remains unfulfilled. If killed, it reforms within 1 day near the object it guards, near someone under a [geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) to fulfill its need, or at the site of its original appearance. Once the guardian's need is fulfilled, the crossroads guardian dissipates naturally within a week unless it finds a new quest. --- **Speed** 25 feet, swim 25 feet **Melee** greatsword +18 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d12+11 slashing plus 1d6 mental **Primal Innate Spells** DC 25 - **4th** [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) (at will), [Solid Fog](/Spells.aspx?ID=290) - **7th** [Plane Shift](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Rituals** DC 25 - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) (doesn't require secondary casters and can target a willing creature of any level)
Variant Guardians A crossroads guardian is always at least partially neutral, but can gain additional alignment traits from the community or task whose need prompted the guardian's creation. They can also have different innate spells and abilities uniquely suited to their specific need.
name: Crossroads Guardian

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dretch (Sloth Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1109)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Among the lowest and least powerful of all demons, dretches are just as vicious and cruel as their more powerful brethren. They happily vent their frustrations on anything weaker than themselves.

 Born from the souls of the slothful, these demons appear vaguely humanoid in appearance with awkward limbs and slack lumps of skin hanging from their frames. Supposedly, a single slothful soul of sufficient depravity and strength can spawn an entire horde of these creatures. However, as weaker demons, their lot in life is to suffer at the hands of more powerful Abyssal predators, and only a tiny number of dretches ever manage to become something more. For this reason, dretches are quite accommodating when summoned by mortals. They're usually content to work under conditions few other outsiders would accept, although they'll still gladly turn on even the kindest of masters who show even the slightest hint of weakness.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Dretch.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Dretch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1109)</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=42" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 60

**Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +6, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +6

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +0

**Con** +4

**Int** -3

**Wis** +0

**Cha** +0
</row>

**Sloth** At the start of each of the dretch's turns, roll 1d4. The result is the number of actions the dretch regains that turn (maximum 3). Effects like the [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) condition can further reduce their number of actions.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +4 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 45

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 3, good 3

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +9 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d8+3 piercing plus 1d4 evil

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d6+3 slashing plus 1d4 evil

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 16
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110)
- **3rd**
[Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=289)

**Rituals** DC 16

- **1st**
[Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21)

**Cower** <actions string="Single Action" />  The dretch makes itself as small as possible, protecting its vital organs with its limbs. It gains a +4 circumstance bonus to AC but takes a –2 penalty to attack rolls. This lasts until the dretch moves from its current space, falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or ends the effect as a free action.

**Frenzied Slashes** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The dretch makes three claw Strikes, each at a –2 penalty, all targeting the same creature. The dretch's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after it has made all three attacks. The dretch gains the [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) condition until the beginning of its next turn.

**Vicious Criticals**   A dretch makes the most of any weakness it finds. Whenever a dretch scores a critical hit with its claw Strike, the target takes an additional 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Abrikandilu (Wrecker Demon)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1110)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Wrecker demons, also known as abrikandilus, despise beautiful things and do everything in their power to destroy both people and objects regarded as such. An abrikandilu forms from the souls mortals who were vandals, misanthropes, or defacers of artwork, particularly if their destructive actions stemmed from feelings of powerful envy.

 An abrikandilu loathes only one thing more than beauty: their own visage. The mere sight of their face—reflected in a mirror, a shield, or even a pool of water— can send a wrecker demon into a rage. Many demon slayers leverage tactic to their advantage, venturing into battle with polished steel shields and cold iron blades at the ready.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 19

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Demon_Abrikandilu.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Abrikandilu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1110)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Demon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=42" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 61

**Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +1

**Con** +3

**Int** -2

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +0
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 19 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 70

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, good 5

**Hatred of Mirrors** An abrikandilu loathes the sight of their reflection. When a creature Interacts with a mirror within sight of the wrecker demon, the demon takes a –2 penalty to Will saves against [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks. An abrikandilu that ends their turn adjacent to a mirror or that's attacked by a creature holding a mirror takes 1d6 mental damage (this usually leads abrikandilus to focus their efforts on destroying nearby mirrors using Wreck).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +14,
**Damage** 3d6+4 piercing plus mutilating bite

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hurled debris +11 ([range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 20
- **2nd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) (×2)

**Rituals** DC 20

- **1st**
[Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21)

**Mutilating Bite**  ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the abrikandilu hits a creature with a jaws Strike, the creature must succeed at a DC 21 Fortitude save or become physically mutilated. The creature then takes a –1 status penalty to Charisma-based checks. This penalty is cumulative up to –3 and remains even if the wounds are healed. The penalty is reduced by 1 every 24 hours until it reaches 0.

**Wreck** <actions string="Single Action" />  The abrikandilu makes two claw Strikes against an unattended object or held mirror. Held mirrors use the holding character's AC. If both Strikes hit, combine their damage for the purpose of overcoming any Hardness or resistance. These Strikes don't count toward the abrikandilu's multiple attack penalty, nor does that penalty apply to these Strikes.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Demons of Vandalism</title>

It is a strange irony that abrikandilus despise all works of artistry, yet without the objects of their jealousy they would have nothing to rage at but themselves.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-28" />
name: Abrikandilu

markdown:
These goat-headed demons have glowing red eyes and flaming hooves but measure only about 3 feet in height. Born from the souls of arsonists, the fiery brimoraks continue the work they pursued in life, as everything they touch quickly burns. As a general rule, brimoraks are ill-tempered even for demons, although their spite turns to glee in the face of a growing fire. Those who have survived encounters with these fiends report that they remember the braying sound of the brimoraks' laughter as clearly as the heat of the flames or the choking scent of smoke. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Brimorak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1111) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 62 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), smoke vision **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 60 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +2 **Extinguishing Aversion** Dousing a brimorak with water, either ordinary water or from a [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) effect, causes no physical harm to the fiend but deals 3d6 mental damage. Fully immersing the brimorak in water deals 5d6 mental damage per round.
**Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair a brimorak's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +12 **Will** +10 **HP** 80 **Immunities** fire **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, good 5 **Boiling Blood** Each time an adjacent creature deals slashing or piercing damage to the brimorak, the attacker is sprayed with the brimorak's boiling blood, which deals 2d4 fire damage (DC 19 basic Reflex save). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** flaming sword +15 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+4 slashing plus 1d6 evil and 1d6 fire **Melee** hoof +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d4+4 bludgeoning plus 1d6 evil and 1d6 fire **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Produce Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=236) - **3rd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=119) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) **Rituals** DC 21 - **1st** [Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21) **Breath Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The brimorak spits their boiling blood in a 20-foot line that deals 6d6 fire damage (DC 21 basic Reflex save). The brimorak can't use their Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. The ground within this area becomes slippery, with the effects of a [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) spell targeting an area until it's cleaned up or the brimorak is able to use their Breath Weapon again, whichever comes first. **Flaming Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) A brimorak's hooves and any weapon they wield burst into flame, dealing an extra 1d6 fire damage with each hit. If they don't have a weapon, they can create a flaming sword of fire and steel (see flaming sword Strike in Melee entry above). **Fume** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The brimorak emits a cloud of thick black smoke in a 10-foot burst that remains in place for 1d4 rounds. All creatures within the smoke become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the smoke become concealed to creatures within it. A creature that enters or begins its turn within the smoke it must succeed at a DC 21 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure).
The Smallest Sparks Many underestimate brimoraks due to their size, forgetting that tiny embers can blow into raging infernos in the blink of an eye. Brimoraks believe that they grow larger with each fire they set, though larger arson demons have yet to be seen.
name: Brimorak

markdown:
Seemingly made from living, animated filth, omoxes have no true anatomy, although they generally spend most of their time in roughly humanoid shapes, resembling some grim caricatures of half-melted humanoids. While scholars once believed these foul demons to be a pure, concentrated form of the corruption that suffuses the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) and its inhabitants, in truth these demons arise from the souls of those who routinely befouled and polluted their surroundings in life. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Omox](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1112) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 63 **Perception** +22; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +24 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +7 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 **Cleanly Vulnerability** An omox embodies filth, and they find the concept of cleanliness abhorrent. An omox subjected to an effect that cleans them, such as the tidy command of [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), takes 2d6 mental damage. They also take this damage the first time each round a creature hit by one of the omox's attacks spends actions cleaning off the filth. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +21 **Will** +20 **HP** 395 **Immunities** acid, critical hits, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10, good 10 **Grab Weapon** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Trigger** A creature hits the omox with a melee weapon; **Effect** The omox attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=41) the triggering creature. On a critical success, the weapon becomes subsumed within the omox's body rather than falling to the ground, from which it must be Disarmed before it can be retrieved. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet, swim 80 feet **Melee** sludge tendril +25, **Damage** 2d6+13 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid and 1d6 evil and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Ranged** slime ball +24 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d4+13 bludgeoning plus 2d6 acid and 1d6 evil and slime trap **Divine Innate Spells** DC 32 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53) (at will), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309) **Rituals** DC 32 - **1st** [Abyssal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=21) **Liquid Leap** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirements** The omox is in a space of liquid; **Effect** The omox teleports from its current space to any unoccupied space of liquid within 120 feet. **Slime Trap** A creature hit by an omox's slime ball must attempt a DC 32 Reflex save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 35).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the target is also [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for the same duration. **Smother** **Requirements** The omox has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20); **Effect** The demon flows over the grabbed creature, covering it in oozing acidic slime. The creature must succeed at a DC 32 Fortitude save or else it becomes [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) and must hold its breath or begin [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). These effects lasts as long as the omox has the creature grabbed.
name: Omox

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hellbound Attorney](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1113)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The Hellbound attorney sought the assistance of a [phistophilus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=112)—one of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18)'s contract devils—to bolster her legalistic ability on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 21

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Devil_Hellbound.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 4">[Hellbound Attorney](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1113)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Devil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=44" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
<trait label="Human" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=90" />
<trait label="Humanoid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=91" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 64

**Perception** +11; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +11, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +14, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +12

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +2

**Con** +0

**Int** +4

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Items**
elegant cane (as [mace](/Weapons.aspx?ID=7)), legal ledgers

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 20 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 60

</row>

**Resistances**
fire 4

**Weaknesses**
good 2

**Abrogation of Consequences** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** The Hellbound attorney rolls a success or critical failure on a saving throw against a [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) effect; **Effect** The attorney finds a loophole in the wording of the effect, turning the success into a critical success or a critical failure into a normal failure.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
elegant cane +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)),
**Damage** 1d4+3 bludgeoning

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 21
- **1st**
[Burning Hands](/Spells.aspx?ID=30)

**Opening Statement** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Trigger** The Hellbound attorney's turn begins; **Effect** The attorney enumerates the alleged crimes of a creature they can see and attempts a [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check against that creature's Will DC. On a success, the attorney's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage (4d6 precision damage on a critical success) to the creature until the end of the attorney's turn.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-30" />
name: Hellbound Attorney

Fearsome giants of jagged iron, levalochs serve in the armies of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18) as potent warriors and tenacious hunters—creatures of absolute discipline endlessly obedient to diabolical tyrants. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 23 **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Levaloch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1114) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 65 **Perception** +16; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +14, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +14 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +1 **Hellstrider** A levaloch ignores the effects of non-magical difficult terrain. They take no damage from [caltrops](/Equipment.aspx?ID=9) or from damaging terrain that deals physical, acid, or cold damage. A levaloch can move through liquids up to 5 feet deep at their full Speed. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +14 **Will** +12 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 105 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), fire, [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 5 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)) **Weaknesses** good 5 **Phalanx Fighter** All devils of equal or lower level adjacent to a levaloch gain a +1 circumstance bonus to their AC as the levaloch shields them from harm.
**Stable Stance** A levaloch gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their Fortitude DC against being [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) and to other saving throws to resist being moved against their will. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet **Melee** _trident_ +19 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing plus 1d6 evil and merciless thrust **Ranged** _trident_ +16 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing plus 1d6 evil **Ranged** barbed net +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** barbed net **Rituals** DC 22 - **1st** [Infernal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=23) **Barbed Net** When a levaloch hits a creature with their barbed net, the net wraps around the target, which becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds. If the Strike was a critical success, the target is also [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). When a creature [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 22), or if the Strike misses, the net crumbles into rust. Each time a creature attempts to Escape, it takes 1d6 slashing damage from the net's barbs, regardless of whether the attempt succeeds. **Forge Weapon** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) A levaloch reforges part of their barbed iron substance into a new _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [trident](/Weapons.aspx?ID=45)_ or barbed net. Their previous trident crumbles to rust. When the levaloch is destroyed, any tridents or barbed nets they created crumble to rust. **Merciless Thrust** When a levaloch hits a creature that has the [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) conditions with a melee trident Strike, the Strike deals an additional 2d6 damage.
Hellforged Construct Levalochs are strange amalgamations of devil and automaton, never requiring food or rest. The engravings on their iron plates indicate their hellish allegiance to a certain [archdevil](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=4), infernal duke, malebranche, or [Queen of the Night](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=14).
name: Levaloch

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Munagola (Executioner Devil)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1115)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Munagolas are some of the most feared demonic warriors in Hell, combining the aerial strength and craftiness of erinyes with sheer brute force rivaling that of the strongest devils.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Devil_Munagola.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 11">[Munagola](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1115)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Devil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=44" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 66

**Perception** +24; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +6

**Con** +6

**Int** +3

**Wis** +5

**Cha** +6
</row>

**Items**
_+1 [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=74)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=21)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 31 

**Fort** +23 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +20 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 165

</row>

**Immunities**
fire

**Resistances**
physical 10 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379))

**Weaknesses**
good 10

**No Escape** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A foe within reach moves away from the munagola; **Effects** The munagola Strides or Flies up to their Speed, following the retreating foe and keeping it in reach until the foe stops moving or the munagola has moved their full Speed.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_flaming greataxe_ +24 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)),
**Damage** 2d12+13 slashing plus 1d6 evil and 1d6 fire

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 piercing plus 1d6 evil

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_flaming composite longbow_ +23 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=201)),
**Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus 1d6 evil and 1d6 fire

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 27
- **4th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will)
- **5th**
[Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69)

**Rituals** DC 27

- **1st**
[Infernal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=23)

**Felling Assault**   When a munagola hits with a melee Strike, they gain [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) on all subsequent melee Strikes against that target until the end of the munagola's turn.

**Flames of Fury**   Any weapon a munagola holds gains the effects of a [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295) rune while they hold it.

**Flight Commander of Dis** <actions string="Single Action" />  The munagola directs aerial maneuvers, granting themself and evil allies with fly Speeds a +20-foot status bonus to their fly Speeds for 1 round.

**Sudden Dive** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The munagola Flies twice. If they end their flight at a lower elevation than they started and within melee reach of at least one enemy, they can make a melee Strike against that enemy, gaining a +1 circumstance bonus to the Strike.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Defying Limiation</title>

While Dis is a city of unyielding order and unforgiving law, the Queen of the Night [Eiseth](/Deities.aspx?ID=169) operates outside of the established diabolical hierarchy. Despite her stronghold being located within the Infernal City, her army of [erinyes](/Monsters.aspx?ID=111), commanded by the fierce and brutal munagolas, answer only to her. They strike her enemies without mercy or restraint, even in open defiance of [Dispater](/Deities.aspx?ID=66) if it suits their queen.

</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-30" />
name: Munagola

markdown:
As masters of cold, calculated logic and perfectly timed proselytization, the loathsome deimaviggas seek to set friend against friend and turn the faithful from their beliefs at every opportunity. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Deimavigga](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1116) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 66 **Perception** +32; (+36 to Sense Motive) greater darkvision **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19); indomitable oration, [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +30, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +30, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +33 **Str** +7 **Dex** +8 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +9 **Indomitable Oration** Any creature capable of comprehending speech understands the deimavigga, as if they constantly spoke in all languages at once. **Items** _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) [glamered](/Equipment.aspx?ID=286) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_ --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +29 **Will** +32 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 285 **Immunities** fire **Resistances** physical 15 (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)) **Weaknesses** good 15 **Whispers of Discord** **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet is targeted by a spell that would restore Hit Points or provide a status bonus (the deimavigga automatically recognizes such effects); **Effect** The deimavigga whispers disturbing lies, audible only to the target, to shake the target's faith in the spell's caster. The target must attempt a DC 43 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target disbelieves the lies and receives the intended benefit of the spell; the target becomes temporarily immune to Whispers of Discord for 24 hours.
**Success** As critical success, but the target isn't temporarily immune.
**Failure** The spell fails to affect the target. The target refuses all aid from that caster for 1 round and doesn't count as the caster's ally.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the duration is 1 minute. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** claw +33 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+15 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Divine Innate Spells** DC 42, attack +34 - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=90) (at will) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **7th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Scrying](/Spells.aspx?ID=268), [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341) (at will), [Veil](/Spells.aspx?ID=355), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=369) - **9th** [Divine Decree](/Spells.aspx?ID=82), [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=161) (at will) **Rituals** DC 43 - **1st** [Infernal Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=23) **Boundless Reach** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) A deimavigga's razor-sharp claws can slice through reality, allowing them to make claw Strikes and use spells with a range of touch against any creature they can see directly or via divination magic. A creature targeted this way can retaliate until the start of the deimavigga's next turn; it can target the devil's claws as if the devil were physically present and adjacent to the target, though the claws are concealed. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The deimavigga can take on the appearance of any [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). This doesn't change their Speed or attack and damage bonuses with Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Whisper Earworm** ([Divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) The deimavigga whispers a terrifying multiversal truth to one adjacent creature, shaking its faith in reality and divinity. The target must attempt a DC 43 Will save. [Celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=23) and [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) gain a +2 status bonus to this save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and temporarily immune to Whisper Earworm for 24 hours.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The next time the target rests, it ruminates on the deimavigga's words instead of sleeping or otherwise resting and awakens [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15). The target also becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) until it's no longer fatigued.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but drained 2. After this rest, the target must attempt another DC 43 Will save. On a failure, the target becomes stupefied 2 and takes a –4 status penalty to Will saves against effects from evil creatures. These effects last until the target unlearns the truth spoken by the deimavigga, requiring a [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) spell, other means of modifying their memory, or powerful magic such as [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377).
Lone Operators While most devils perform a specialized role within a complex infernal machine, deimaviggas work almost exclusively alone, as they find even the most obedient minions a hindrance to their stratagems.
name: Deimavigga

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Doru](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1117)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

These deceivers whisper fetid lies, sowing dark and dread inspiration into foolish mortal ears. Dorus serve as the spies and messengers of the divs. With silver tongues and dark motives, they often pledge themselves to vainglorious and deluded spellcasters, who they ply with wild conspiracies and rumors of deception among friends and foes alike; in the process, they push supposed masters to despotism and murderous revenge. All the while, dorus wean secrets, and weaknesses, from their marks. In the end, nearly every doru turns on their false liege, destroying the reputation and even causing the death of the person the doru pretended to serve.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Div_Doru.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Doru](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1117)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Div" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=351" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 68

**Perception** +7; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) (touch)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +6, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +10, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +4

**Con** +1

**Int** +3

**Wis** +2

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 16 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +7 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 20

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 3, good 3

**Covetous of Secrets** Dorus have a weakness for secrets, hoarding them like a miser hoards gold. A creature can tempt a doru with some bit of obscure knowledge the doru doesn't know or thinks they don't know. Presenting the hint of the secret is a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) and [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) traits, and requires a skill check using [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) (or some other appropriate skill determined by the GM) against the doru's Will DC. On a success, the doru is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) for as long as the presenter draws out the explanation of the secret (spending 1 action each round doing so, to a maximum of 1 minute). On a critical success, the doru is fascinated for that duration plus 1 minute more as it ponders the implications of the secret. Regardless of the outcome, the doru is temporarily immune to that creature's attempts to present it with secrets for 1 day.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, fly 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 1d6 piercing plus 1 evil and doru venom

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66)
- **1st**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160)
- **2nd**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (at will; self only)
- **4th**
[Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247)

**Doru Venom**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** DC 16 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 round); **Stage 3** 1d6 poison and stupefied 2 (1 round).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Doru Secrets</title>

Despite dorus' obsession, not all secrets capture their focus—instead, each fixates on a unique topic. Many are interested in the history of a particular mortal ancestry, while others delve into riddles, mathematical puzzles, or even local gossip.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-225" />
name: Doru
