markdown:
Walking embodiments of curses, misfortune, and the evil eye, aghashes wander the deserts of the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), searching out the arrogant, charming, and persuasive to humiliate and undermine. Aghashes are often mistaken for some strange form of [hag](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=59), and like those creatures, they're masters at curses. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Aghash](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1118) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 69 **Perception** +12; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +9, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +8, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 19 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +10 **Will** +12 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 75 **Immunities** [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38) **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, good 5 **Hatred of Beauty** While aghashes hate all mortals, they particularly despise beautiful objects and beautiful or charismatic mortals. When not in physical peril, an aghash is compelled to destroy art and other works of beauty. An aghash can't enter an area of pristine beauty without first marring it in some way. Given a choice, an aghash attacks a foe with the highest Charisma score first. If barred from doing so by force or some magical effect, they take 1d6 mental damage at the end of their turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 1d6+5 slashing plus 1d6 evil **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **1st** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160) (at will) - **2nd** [Touch of Idiocy](/Spells.aspx?ID=341) (at will) - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) **Rituals** DC 21 - **1st** [Div Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=46) **Cursed Gaze** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The aghash fixes their gaze on one creature they can see within 20 feet. The creature must attempt a DC 21 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 2d6 mental damage and becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature takes 4d6 mental damage and becomes either frightened 2 or [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) (the aghash's choice).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 8d6 mental damage and becomes frightened 2 and stunned 2. **Sandstorm** ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The aghash creates a temporary sandstorm in a 30 foot emanation that lasts for 1 minute. Creatures within the emanation take a –4 circumstance penalty to Perception checks and must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save. On a failure, they're forced to hold their breath or else they start [suffocating](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). A creature within the sandstorm at the end of its turn takes 1d6 slashing damage. [Divs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=225) are immune to all effects of an aghash's sandstorm.
name: Aghash

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Pairaka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1119)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Often charming, sometimes even seductive, pairakas worm their way into mortal relationships, subtly destroying the ties of friendship and love through emotional and physical corruption and plague. Pairakas appear as beautiful, blue-skinned humanoids spotted with large rashes and boils, but they rarely take on their true forms. Instead, they adopt shapes that attract just enough attention for them to worm their way into the confidences of those they wish to torment and corrupt.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Div_Pairaka.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Pairaka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1119)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Div" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=351" />
<trait label="Fiend" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=70" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 70

**Perception** +15; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +14, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +7
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +12 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +17 

+1 status to all saves vs. magic
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 105

</row>

**Immunities**
[disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, good 5

**Hatred of Red** Pairakas hate the color red. They won't wear the color or willingly enter any place painted red. Given a choice, they'll attack a creature wearing red first. If barred from expressing their displeasure toward the color by force or some magical effect, they take 2d6 mental damage at the end of their turn.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 2d8+6 slashing plus 1d6 evil and bubonic plague

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 25
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66)
- **4th**
[Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34) (at will), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Misdirection](/Spells.aspx?ID=198) (at will; self only), [Outcast's Curse](/Spells.aspx?ID=211) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will)

**Rituals** DC 25

- **1st**
[Div Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=46)

**Bubonic Plague**  ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) A creature can't remove the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition while infected; **Saving Throw** DC 23 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 day; **Stage 1** fatigued (1 day); **Stage 2** [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and fatigued (1 day); **Stage 3** enfeebled 3, fatigued, and take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) every 1d20 minutes (1 day)

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=8)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157), [Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) The pairaka can take the appearance of any Small or Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) or [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=9). This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with the Strikes, but it might change the damage type their strikes deal.

**Tormenting Dreams** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The pairaka torments a sleeping creature within 100 feet with visions of betrayals by loved ones and friends. The target must attempt a DC 25 Will save, with the effects of the [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) spell.

</column>

markdown:
Sepids are the brutal warlords of the divs, delighting in violence against mortals and their own particular flavor of vengeance. Inveterate liars, sepids can't help but spout the most outrageous lies, and their form and nature represent the power of falsehoods to snowball into violence that can cost countless lives. Among mortals, they rally troops to incite rebellion, war, and other forms of carnage, savoring mortals who slaughter each other. These giant beings usually stand around 13 feet tall; they wade through battlefields seeking out heroes and generals, rejoicing maniacally as they cut their foes down. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Sepid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1120) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 71 **Perception** +24; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +23, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +28, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +23 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [falchion](/Weapons.aspx?ID=18)_ --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +28 **Ref** +23 **Will** +20 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 350 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10, good 10 **Blatant Liar** While all divs delight in lying, sepids are compulsive and predictable liars who always do the opposite of what they claim they'll do. If a sepid is ever forced or compelled to tell the truth, they take 4d8 mental damage.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** A sepid gains an extra reaction each round that they can use only to make an Attack of Opportunity.
**Deflecting Lie** **Trigger** A creature hits the sepid with a ranged Strike or a ranged spell attack roll; **Effect** The sepid lies in an attempt to divert the attack. They roll a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) check against the triggering creature's Perception DC. On a success, if the triggering attack roll was a success, it becomes a failure, and if the triggering attack roll was a critical hit, it becomes a normal success. --- **Speed** 35 feet **Melee** _falchion_ +30 ([Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194)), **Damage** 2d10+16 slashing plus 1d6 evil and 1d6 mental **Melee** claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus 1d6 evil and 1d6 mental **Divine Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **3rd** [Comprehend Language](/Spells.aspx?ID=46) (at will; self only) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59) (at will), [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125) - **7th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Misdirection](/Spells.aspx?ID=198) (at will; self only), [Nondetection](/Spells.aspx?ID=209) (at will; self only), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) **Rituals** DC 34 - **1st** [Div Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=46) - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=10) (no secondary caster required) **Rain of Debris** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The sepid calls forth a hail of stone, wood, metal, and similar debris in a 40-foot emanation, dealing 10d6 bludgeoning damage and 5d6 evil damage. Each creature in the area other than the sepid must attempt a basic DC 31 Reflex saving throw. The sepid can't use Rain of Debris again for 1d4 rounds.
Sepid Deceptions Given that sepids always do the opposite of what they say they will, it might seem difficult for these divs to deceive anyone, which is far from the truth. Often, sepids avoid making statements about their own intentions and instead give orders or speak in analogies, riddles, and anecdotes, allowing those they manipulate to parse out their rationales with clever forms of deception.
name: Sepid

This divine warden serves [Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12), the neutral god of magic. It uses its divine gift to defend temples or shrines of Nethys. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Divine Warden Of Nethys](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1121) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 73 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +14 **Str** +5 **Dex** -2 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +9 **Will** +11 **HP** 60 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Divine Destruction** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the divine warden is reduced to 0 HP, it erupts with divine energy in a 30-foot emanation, dealing 5d6 negative damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 19 Will save with the following outcomes.
**Critical Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Success** The creature takes full damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes temporarily cursed by the patron deity. The creature becomes [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 day; this is a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38) effect that uses the Will save DC as the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature becomes enfeebled 2 and stupefied 2
**Faith Bound** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) A divine warden can't attack a creature that openly wears or displays the religious symbol of the divine warden's patron deity unless that creature uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the divine warden first. If the divine warden is intelligent, it can also attack a creature it believes isn't faithful to its deity or who wears the religious symbol as a ruse (typically after succeeding at a Perception check to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=85)). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** fist +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** staff +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 19 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61) **Divine Domain Spells** DC 19, 1 Focus Point - **3rd** [Cry of Destruction](/Spells.aspx?ID=410), [Protector's Sacrifice](/Spells.aspx?ID=441) **Faithful Weapon** A divine warden always wields its patron diety's favored weapon, in this case a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_. **Instrument of Faith** The divine warden is a beacon for its deity's faith. A [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) of the divine warden's patron deity can channel a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell through a divine warden they can see within 60 feet. The cleric determines any targets or area for the spell as if they were standing in the divine warden's space.
name: Divine Warden Of Nethys

markdown:
Forest dragons oversee the endless growth of nature, protect it from plunder, and reclaim ruins for the wilderness. Some say these dragons jealously hoard nature's bounty for themselves while seeing everyone else who tries to benefit from nature as thieves and poachers. They loathe the progress of technology and civilization, preferring the company of monsters and animals. Forest dragons are their forests in a literal sense. Over time, their hair becomes mossy, their hide toughens like bark, and small insects or animals live within them. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 29 **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Young Forest Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1122) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 74 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +16 **Will** +20 **HP** 195 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** fire 5 **Countered by Metal** If the forest dragon takes damage from a metal item, they lose woodland stride and [_pass without trace_](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) until the end of their next turn.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 27. [Animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9), [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=77), and [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) take a –2 circumstance penalty to the save.
**Fed by Water** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** The forest dragon is targeted with a [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) spell or effect; **Effect** The forest dragon gains 30 temporary Hit Points. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet; woodland stride **Melee** jaws +23 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing plus 1d6 poison **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+12 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +23 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+12 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Know Direction](/Spells.aspx?ID=169) - **2nd** [Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103) - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) ([animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9); [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=77); and [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) only) - **Constant (1st)** [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215) (forest terrain only) **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The dragon unleashes a swarm of insects that deals 10d6 piercing damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 29 basic Reflex save) before dispersing. A creature that critically fails is [stunned 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) from the insects' venom; this is a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) effect. The dragon can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+8 bludgeoning, DC 29 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Breath Weapon whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Woodland Stride** The forest dragon ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain from non-magical foliage.
name: Young Forest Dragon

Water is also consumed in the same way, typically lowering any standing body of water fully within the area by 1 foot. **Forest Shape** When casting [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=342), a forest dragon can become a tree of the same size and age as themself. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 4d8+10 bludgeoning, DC 41 **Lignifying Bite** ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a creature made of flesh is reduced to 0 Hit Points by the dragon's jaws Strike, that creature must attempt a DC 41 Fortitude save. If it fails, it dies, and its flesh, made of earth in a metaphysical sense, turns to wood. The wood is living and might grow into a tree over time, but it can't be used as a body or piece of a body for [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=243) or other magic that would return the creature to life. **Woodland Stride** The forest dragon ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain from non-magical foliage.
name: Ancient Forest Dragon

markdown:
Sea dragons' affinity with water grants them mastery over rain, storms, and floods. Those whose livelihoods are at the mercy of the weather and waves frequently venerate these great aquatic creatures. Most sea dragons respond kindly to requests, but, like the sea, they can be unpredictable and fickle. Sea dragons have disk-shaped scales like fish and webbed claws as well as large back and tail fins to aid in swimming. They appear in vivid and vibrant colors, like aquatic wildlife. Underwater flora and fauna grow and thrive upon older sea dragons, and ancient sea dragons often have a head of coral around their antlers, giving them the image of being crowned royalty. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Sea Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1125) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 77 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=41) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 27 fed by metal **Fort** +17 **Ref** +18 **Will** +15 **HP** 140 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Countered by Earth** If the sea dragon is targeted with an [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) spell or [_flesh to stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=123), they lose Liquefy until the end of their next turn.
**Fed by Metal** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When a sea dragon is struck by a weapon made primarily of metal or affected by a spell that uses metal, many of the dragon's scales transform to mimic the metal. This grants the dragon a +1 item bonus to AC until the start of their next turn.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** (aura, emotion, fear, mental) 90 feet, DC 24
**Liquefy** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted by a Strike or spell that could deal fire or physical damage to them; **Effect** The dragon liquefies, turning entirely to water while maintaining their shape, for a split second. Against the triggering effect, they gain resistance 15 to fire and to all physical damage. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 50 feet; [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Melee** jaws +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus hyponatremia **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +20 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 26 - **1st** [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53) (at will) - **2nd** [Endure Elements](/Spells.aspx?ID=99) (self only) - **Constant (2nd)** [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=371) **Breath Weapon** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The dragon shoots a ball of water that deals 9d6 bludgeoning damage in a 20-foot burst within 40 feet (DC 26 basic Reflex save). All non-magical fire in the radius is extinguished. The dragon can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+6 bludgeoning, DC 26 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Breath Weapon whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Hyponatremia** ([Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) A living creature that takes damage from the sea dragon's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) as a surge of excess water floods its body.
name: Young Sea Dragon

markdown:
Sky dragons live above the clouds near the summits of mountains. They maintain a strong religious tradition, a unique practice among imperial dragons that sets them apart as much as their wings do. Desperate souls risk steep cliffs and loose snow to seek these dragons' counsel, and most sky dragons gladly help those who make the journey, but they expel any express hostilities or badger without hesitation. The sky dragon's wings aren't true wings, but long fins fused to their forearms; their scales also form only very slowly. Young dragons coil tightly in their lairs as they absorb minerals that color and harden their scales. Even adults often wrap around pillars out of habit. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Young Sky Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1128) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 79 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Mist Vision** Fog and mist don't impair a sky dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from fog and mist. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +16 **Will** +21 **HP** 155 **Immunities** electricity, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Countered by Fire** If the sky dragon takes fire damage, the elemental magic of metal within them is tempered. Until the end of their next turn, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and AC, and their jaws Strikes don't deal electricity damage. This limitation ends if the dragon uses Breath Weapon.
**Fed by Earth** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) When a sky dragon is targeted by an [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) spell or effect, they leave a cloud of pulverized dust and gravel that swirls in the eddies of the dragon's flight. For 1 minute, the dragon is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while flying.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 26 --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing plus 1d12 electricity **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+12 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +21 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+12 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 28 - **1st** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) **Breath Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The dragon shoots a ball of electricity that deals 5d12 electricity damage in a 20-foot burst within 40 feet (DC 28 basic Reflex save). summary_markdown: Sky dragons live above the clouds near the summits of mountains. They maintain a strong religious tradition, a unique practice among imperial dragons …

markdown:
Sky dragons live above the clouds near the summits of mountains. They maintain a strong religious tradition, a unique practice among imperial dragons that sets them apart as much as their wings do. Desperate souls risk steep cliffs and loose snow to seek these dragons' counsel, and most sky dragons gladly help those who make the journey, but they expel any express hostilities or badger without hesitation. The sky dragon's wings aren't true wings, but long fins fused to their forearms; their scales also form only very slowly. Young dragons coil tightly in their lairs as they absorb minerals that color and harden their scales. Even adults often wrap around pillars out of habit. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Adult Sky Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1129) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 80 **Perception** +24; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +26, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +26, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +21, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Mist Vision** Fog and mist don't impair a sky dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from fog and mist. --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +21 **Will** +26 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 235 **Immunities** electricity, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Countered by Fire** If the sky dragon takes fire damage, the elemental magic of metal within them is tempered. Until the end of their next turn, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and AC, and their jaws Strikes don't deal electricity damage. This limitation ends if the dragon uses Breath Weapon.
**Fed by Earth** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) When a sky dragon is targeted by an [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) spell or effect, they leave a cloud of pulverized dust and gravel that swirls in the eddies of the dragon's flight. For 1 minute, the dragon is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) while flying.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 31 --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 140 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+13 piercing plus 1d12 electricity **Melee** claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+13 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d8+13 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 33 - **1st** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) - **4th** [Gaseous Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=129) **Breath Weapon** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) The dragon shoots a ball of electricity that deals 7d12 electricity damage in a 25-foot burst within 50 feet (DC 33 basic Reflex save). They can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. See divine lightning below. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target. **Divine Lightning** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) A good sky dragon who worships a deity channels divine power through its attacks, making them more effective against [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). Any electricity damage it deals becomes good damage against fiends or positive damage against undead. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Breath Weapon whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 3d8+8 bludgeoning, DC 33 **Perfected Flight** A sky dragon can hover in place without spending an action, and they automatically succeed at all [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30).
name: Adult Sky Dragon

summary_markdown: Sky dragons live above the clouds near the summits of mountains. They maintain a strong religious tradition, a unique practice among imperial dragons …

markdown:
The most well-known of imperial dragons due to their deep involvement with mortals, the sovereign dragons' name comes from their mandate of selecting rulers—but at some point, the dragons themselves joined the list of worthy candidates. Other imperial dragons suspect the sovereign dragons offered their connection to the elemental cycle to attain forbidden magic, evidenced by their lack of any vulnerability to wood or ability to feed on fire, despite being creatures of the earth. Most hesitate to question the sovereigns' authority. While some appreciate their impartial nature, others fault this adherence to neutrality. Regardless, the sovereign dragons' charm and centuries of experience in subtle manipulation have made them exceptional negotiators. Almost all sovereign dragons appear gold in color. Their hair ranges from common colors found among humans to bright reds, greens, or blues. They're the only dragons with five digits per claw, a mark of special importance. Other imperial dragons dismiss this claim to status and instead tease the sovereign dragons over their need to wear armor, even while in their draconic forms. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Young Sovereign Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1131) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 81 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +18 **Will** +24 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 195 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** mental 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 28
**Violent Retort** **Trigger** A creature within the sovereign dragon's reach critically hits the dragon; **Effect** The sovereign dragon makes a claw or tail Strike against the creature, after applying all the effects of the critical hit to the dragon. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+13 piercing plus 2d6 mental **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +24 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30 - **2nd** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; good or evil only) - **6th** [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will) **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The dragon unleashes a roar charged with psychic energy, dealing 12d6 mental damage in a 30-foot cone (DC 30 basic Will save). The dragon can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+10 bludgeoning, DC 26 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Breath Weapon whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Gleaming Armor** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The dragon's golden armor glows with a protective golden light. The dragon gains a +2 status bonus to AC and resistance 10 to energy damage. Each enemy in a 20-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7). All these effects last until the end of the dragon's next turn.
name: Young Sovereign Dragon

summary_markdown: The most well-known of imperial dragons due to their deep involvement with mortals, the sovereign dragons' name comes from their mandate of selecting …

markdown:
The most well-known of imperial dragons due to their deep involvement with mortals, the sovereign dragons' name comes from their mandate of selecting rulers—but at some point, the dragons themselves joined the list of worthy candidates. Other imperial dragons suspect the sovereign dragons offered their connection to the elemental cycle to attain forbidden magic, evidenced by their lack of any vulnerability to wood or ability to feed on fire, despite being creatures of the earth. Most hesitate to question the sovereigns' authority. While some appreciate their impartial nature, others fault this adherence to neutrality. Regardless, the sovereign dragons' charm and centuries of experience in subtle manipulation have made them exceptional negotiators. Almost all sovereign dragons appear gold in color. Their hair ranges from common colors found among humans to bright reds, greens, or blues. They're the only dragons with five digits per claw, a mark of special importance. Other imperial dragons dismiss this claim to status and instead tease the sovereign dragons over their need to wear armor, even while in their draconic forms. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Ancient Sovereign Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1133) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 83 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Ignan](/Languages.aspx?ID=18), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Terran](/Languages.aspx?ID=22) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +31, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +37, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +41, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +31, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +31, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +34 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** +5 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +9 --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +31 **Will** +36 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 410 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** mental 20 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 41
**Mentalist Counterspell** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted by a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect it's aware of; **Effect** The dragon immediately attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering effect (counteract modifier +32).
**Violent Retort** **Trigger** A creature within the sovereign dragon's reach critically hits the dragon; **Effect** The sovereign dragon makes a claw or tail Strike against the creature, after applying all the effects of the critical hit to the dragon. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 piercing plus 3d6 mental **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 43 - **2nd** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; good or evil only) - **7th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315) (at will) - **9th** [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=365) **Breath Weapon** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The dragon unleashes a roar charged with psychic energy, dealing 21d6 mental damage in a 50-foot cone (DC 43 basic Will save). The dragon can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Breath Weapon whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Gleaming Armor** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The dragon's golden armor glows with a protective golden light. The dragon gains a +2 status bonus to AC and resistance 20 to energy damage. Each enemy in a 20-foot emanation must succeed at a DC 43 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7). All these effects last until the end of the dragon's next turn. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 4d8+11 bludgeoning, DC 43 **Inspire Envoy** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon chooses a mortal they've observed performing an exceptional act and offers their august blessing in exchange for the creature carrying out a specific directive, such as defeating a tyrant or protecting a sacred site. If the creature agrees, until its next daily preparations, it gains a +2 status bonus to the dragon's choice of attack rolls, AC, or all of the following: Perception, Will saves, and Charisma-based skill checks. The dragon can Dismiss this benefit by spending a single action (that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait), which they're swift to do if the mortal dares to defy the dragon's directive.
name: Ancient Sovereign Dragon

summary_markdown: The most well-known of imperial dragons due to their deep involvement with mortals, the sovereign dragons' name comes from their mandate of selecting …

markdown:
Underworld dragons embody the pressure between tectonic plates, the heat behind geysers, and flash fires that ignite when the conditions are just right. Natural alchemists, they innately understand reagents and catalysts. While some research the effects of magic on material reactions, the majority of underworld dragons concentrate on building their hoards. They scour the world for carved gemstones and artifacts of precious minerals, both of which they see as distilled essence of earth's bounty. Underworld dragons appear angular and sharp; their scales glow as if superheated from within, and their hair flickers like tongues of flames. An intense fire glows behind their gazes, suggesting they could ignite items with a mere thought, and their breath ripples the air as a testament to the heat contained within their bodies. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Young Underworld Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1134) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 83 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair an underworld dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +15 **HP** 115 **Immunities** fire, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Countered by Water** If the underworld dragon takes damage from a spell with the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait, the elemental magic of fire within them is momentarily dampened. Until the end of their next turn, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, and their jaws Strikes don't deal fire damage. This limitation ends if the dragon uses Breath Weapon.
**Fed by Wood** When an underworld dragon is struck by a weapon made primarily of wood or affected by a spell with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) trait, the dragon's internal fiery essences are stoked with the added fuel. Their breath weapon recharges, and the fire damage from the dragon's jaws increases by one die until the end of the dragon's next turn.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 23 --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 30 feet, fly 80 feet **Melee** jaws +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus 1d6 fire **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing plus adamantine claws and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +18 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+8 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) - **2nd** [Continual Flame](/Spells.aspx?ID=50) **Adamantine Claws** The dragon's claws are infused with [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374). Their claw Strikes ignore half the Hardness of any object hit. **Breath Weapon** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The dragon breathes a blast of fire that explodes in a 20-foot burst within 40 feet, dealing 7d6 fire damage (DC 25 basic Reflex save). Creatures that fail the save also take 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The dragon can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** 2d8+4 bludgeoning, DC 25 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Breath Weapon whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.
name: Young Underworld Dragon

The dragon can't use Breath Weapon again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon automatically [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) the target. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Breath Weapon whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=19)** 3d10+9 bludgeoning, DC 37 **Scorch Earth** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) The dragon plunges their body into the ground, Burrows up to their Speed, and then scorches the land within a 60-foot emanation. Creatures underground or on the surface in the area take 8d6 fire damage (DC 37 basic Fortitude save), and those who critically fail become [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 minute.
name: Ancient Underworld Dragon

markdown:
Imbued with the divine energy of the goddess of clockwork and invention, divine wardens of [Brigh](/Deities.aspx?ID=33) are prominent in Alkenstar as guardians of the Temple of Brigh there. The Bronze Lady might have her largest following in this metropolis. But the city's boomtown atmosphere means that security is a concern even on holy ground. Citizens and visitors to Alkenstar know about its wind-up sentries or quickly learn about them. The High Clockmother, head priestess of Brigh's holy place, keeps divine wardens in the goddess's image visible in the sanctuary and on the grounds. These automatons are as much a warning to would-be thieves or troublemakers as they are an inspiration to Brigh's worshippers. The mixing of divine and arcane in these constructs is intended to show off innovation and evoke Brigh's power over earth, fire, metal, and smoke. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Divine Warden Of Brigh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1137) **Source** [Pathfinder Bestiary 3 Bonus Monster](/Sources.aspx?ID=78) **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +18 **Str** +2 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -5 **Wind-Up** For a divine warden of Brigh to act, it must be wound with a unique key by another creature. This takes 1 minute. Once wound, it remains operational for 24 hours, after which time it becomes unaware of its surroundings and can't act until it's wound again. The divine warden shuts down immediately once it has 0 time remaining.
A divine warden can enter standby mode as a 3-action activity. Its operational time doesn't decrease in standby, but it can sense its surroundings (with a –2 penalty to Perception). It can't act, with one exception: when it perceives a creature, it can exit standby as a reaction (rolling initiative if appropriate).
A creature can attempt to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) to wind a divine warden down (DC 27). For each success, the warden loses 1 hour of operational time. This can be done even if the warden is in standby mode. **Items** _clockwork wand_ --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +20 **Will** +18 **HP** 135 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poisoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or orichalcum) **Weaknesses** electricity 10, [orichalcum](/Equipment.aspx?ID=378) 10 **Clockwork Wand** The divine warden uses a mechanical wand as a focus to channel magical energy. This wand is built into the divine warden's chest, with only the crystal at the end exposed. The warden can Interact to remove the wand, or someone else can remove it with a DC 32 [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=69). The divine warden becomes unable to cast any spells except cantrips while the wand is removed.
When removed, the _clockwork wand_ is a magic wand containing the last 2nd-level innate spell the divine warden cast (the GM determines the spell randomly if the divine warden has not cast any eligible spells). The spells are placed within the wand while the divine warden is created, and the creator can substitute other arcane spells of the appropriate level.
**Divine Destruction** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the divine warden is reduced to 0 HP, it erupts with divine energy in a 30-foot emanation, dealing 10d6 positive damage. Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 29 Will save with the following outcomes.
**Critical Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Success** The creature takes full damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes temporarily cursed by Brigh to be [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 day; this is a [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38) effect with a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC of 29.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature becomes enfeebled 2 and stupefied 2.
**Faith Bound** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) The divine warden can't attack a creature that openly wears or displays Brigh's religious symbol unless that creature uses a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the divine warden first. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** fist +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 2d10+6 bludgeoning **Melee** light hammer +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning **Ranged** light hammer +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=280), [Tanglefoot](/Spells.aspx?ID=330) - **1st** [Feather Fall](/Spells.aspx?ID=111), [Floating Disk](/Spells.aspx?ID=124), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140) - **2nd** [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [Web](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) - **3rd** [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Stinking Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=309) - **4th** [Dimensional Anchor](/Spells.aspx?ID=70), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=362) - **5th** [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42), [Cone of Cold](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61) **Divine Domain Spells** 2 Focus Points, DC 29, attack +21 - **5th** [Hurtling Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=427), [Localized Quake](/Spells.aspx?ID=429) **Energize Clockwork Wand** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The divine warden regains a spell it has already cast that day. It must spend 1 hour of its operational time, or 2 hours if the spell is 3rd level or higher. **Faithful Weapon** [light hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=29), [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) rune **Instrument of Faith** The divine warden is a beacon for [Brigh's](/Deities.aspx?ID=33) faith. A [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=5) of Brigh can channel a [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell through a divine warden of Brigh they can see within 60 feet. The cleric determines any targets or area for the spell as if they were standing in the divine warden's space.
name: Divine Warden Of Brigh

Beings of inscrutable intent and incredible power, dramofirs hail from the [Dreamlands](/Planes.aspx?ID=22) and shamble across the multiverse in search of dreams to incorporate into their voluminous robes. Few people on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) dare trifle with dramofirs and instead treat them with respect, hoping that they'll simply take their chosen victims' dreams and then go away. Often, this is exactly what happens. Every now and then, however, in the process of siphoning an individual's dreams, the dramofir slays their victim without warning or apparent cause. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 34 **[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Dramofir](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1138) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 86 **Perception** +28; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet; [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +26, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [Dreamlands Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +28 **Str** +3 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +1 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +26 **Will** +30 **HP** 290 **Immunities** [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** surreal anatomy **Weaknesses** slashing 10 **Surreal Anatomy** A dramofir has resistance 10 to precision damage and the extra damage from critical hits. If the resistance is greater than the extra damage, it reduces the extra damage to 0 but doesn't reduce the attack's normal damage. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 25 feet **Melee** needle +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d6+9 piercing plus 2d6 mental **Ranged** needle +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 3d6+9 piercing plus 2d6 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 36 - **4th** [Dimension Door](/Spells.aspx?ID=69) (at will) - **7th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=59), [Mask of Terror](/Spells.aspx?ID=185), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=208), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288), [Soothe](/Spells.aspx?ID=291) (×2) - **8th** [Dream Council](/Spells.aspx?ID=89) - **Constant (5th)** [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Bittersweet Dreams** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The dramofir swishes their robes in a mesmerizing display that brings the dreams in the robes to life before the viewer's very eyes. Each creature within 30 feet must attempt a DC 34 Will save or be filled with ennui.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** For 1 round, the creature takes a –1 status penalty to attack rolls, saving throws, and skill checks, and all other [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effects on it are suppressed.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is also [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) and the duration is 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature is also slowed 1, the status penalty is –2, and the duration is 1 minute. **Pluck Dream** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** The dramofir is adjacent to an [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) or sleeping creature; **Effect** The dramofir licks the creature's forehead, places their hand in the same spot, and siphons the creature's dreams to incorporate into their robes. If the target is affected by any [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect altering their dreams, such as an [animate dream's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=546) curse of endless nightmares or a [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) spell, the dramofir attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) that effect (counteract modifier +26). Otherwise, the dramofir plucks a benign dream from the creature, and the target must attempt a DC 36 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) until the next time it gets a full night's rest.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but stupefied 2, and the dramofir casts a 6th-level [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=200) spell on the target.
Animate Dream Tamers [Animate dreams](/Monsters.aspx?ID=546) despise dramofirs, who in turn regard animate dreams with, strangely, an emotion akin to pity. Communities plagued by an animate dream sometimes search out and beseech a dramofir to put an end to the terrifying menace.
name: Dramofir

markdown:
Duendes fancy themselves as lords of the land—which is true, in a literal sense, these spirits embody the very soil upon the ground. Unlike most other fey, duendes have learned from the society that grew around them. They've come to accept the mortal understanding of what being a landlord means, so they expect rent from their perceived tenants. However, their notions of acceptable rent—and the acceptable role of a landlord for that matter—don't really line up with those of most societies. Duendes' natural abilities allow them to dwell in termite mounds, but as societies arose, they adopted the habit of sleeping in their tenants' homes. This tendency can be bothersome, as duendes don't clean up after themselves, lock doors behind them, or have the basic decency to be visible. On top of that, they act like they own the places they settle, casting spells on anyone they find annoying. These quirks are usually tolerated, though, as keeping duende happy means that crops grow well and that their magic protects the families in their care. Families aware of their presence are careful to warn invisible duendes before doing things like tossing bathwater out the window or entering a seemingly empty room. These warnings are usually polite but firm, in the vein of “Please move aside!” More prone to making trouble or being a nuisance than to violence, duendes can still prove dangerous if they feel they have no other recourse. Each one carries a toenail cutter, with full awareness that they can use it to cut other things. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Duende](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1139) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 87 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=5), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11) **Skills** [Farming Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +7, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +8, [Labor Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +7, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +7, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +9 **Str** +1 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** toenail cutter --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +9 **Will** +9 **HP** 35 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** toenail cutter +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Backstabber](/Traits.aspx?ID=172), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d6+3 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 18, attack +10 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=142), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) (self only) - **3rd** [Locate](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) **Earthmound Dweller** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The duende shrinks to the size of an ant, allowing them to enter very small tunnels within the earth such as anthills or termite mounds. Their Speed decreases to 5 feet. They can remain in this size until they end the effect by spending a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.
If the duende is underground and a creature hits the space they're occupying with a Strike (hitting automatically) that deals 5 damage or more, the duende is expelled from the tunnels, returns to their normal size, and takes bludgeoning damage equal to the damage from the Strike. **Fool's Gold** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=92)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The duende is holding brown or yellow earthen objects, such as ginger or animal dung; **Effect** The duende makes the objects look, feel, and smell like gold trinkets of a similar size. A creature that touches or Interacts with an affected object can attempt a DC 18 Perception check to disbelieve the illusion, and a successful DC 20 check with [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) or an appropriate [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill (such as Mining Lore) reveals their true nature. The effect ends if the objects are exposed to sunlight.
Unconventional Payments Duendes don't hold mortal currencies in high regard. They strongly prefer toenail clippings over gold. If they don't receive them often enough, they enter their tenants' homes and help themselves. They aren't very good at doing so and occasionally cut off entire toes during collections.
name: Duende

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Dybbuk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1140)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The discorporated spirits called dybbuks arise from souls denied respite in the afterlife, often because they committed some great transgression in life. A priest can appease dybbuks by helping them achieve some esoteric mission, though these tricksters are prone to deceiving anyone who offers them aid. Only by trapping, cowing, or destroying such spirits can a mortal hope to drive them back. Dybbuks hide within household objects or possessed victims. They particularly enjoy taking over the body of someone their target loves and then slowly destroying the lives of both.

<column gap="tiny">

**Immunities**

**Resistances**
all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128), double resistance against non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103))

**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet, DC 33

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
pain touch +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)),
**Damage** 3d10+14 negative plus 2d6 evil

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +30
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334)
- **3rd**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) (at will)
- **4th**
[Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200)
- **5th**
[Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=36) (×2), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110)
- **6th**
[Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=87), [Feeblemind](/Spells.aspx?ID=112), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=333) (at will)

**Inhabit Object** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129)) The dybbuk possesses a Large or smaller unattended object within 20 feet, making it an [animated object](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=4). This animated object's level can be no higher than the dybbuk's level – 2. This possession ends when the object is destroyed or the dybbuk leaves it by using this ability again. At this point, the dybbuk reappears in the object's square and can't Inhabit an Object again for 1d4 rounds. If the object is destroyed, the dybbuk takes 5d6 force damage.

**Malevolent Possession** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Possession](/Traits.aspx?ID=129)) The dybbuk attempts to possess an adjacent corporeal creature. This has the same effect as the [_possession_](/Spells.aspx?ID=225) spell (DC 34) with an unlimited duration, except since the dybbuk doesn't have a physical body, they aren't [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), and aren't [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28) when the effect ends, though they take 5d6 force damage if the body is knocked unconscious or killed. If the dybbuk took control of the target with Malevolent Possession, when the dybbuk departs, the target has only incoherent memories of the interval it was possessed. If a creature dies while possessed by the dybbuk and its corpse remains intact, the dybbuk can immediately inhabit the corpse (see Inhabit Object) as a reaction, in which case the corpse becomes an animated object.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Cruel Puppet Masters</title>

Dybbuks revel in tricking mortals and using their telekinetic abilities to sow fear in the hearts of those around them. As if possessing innocents wasn't horrible enough, dybbuks can also continue to control their unwitting puppets even after their hosts have been slain, animating the corpses like macabre marionettes.
</aside>
name: Dybbuk

markdown:
Einherjar are mighty warriors chosen by valkyries from the ranks of those slain in terrible and legendary battles. Forged from the souls of the greatest warriors, the implacable einherjar serve as the foot soldiers of pantheons, skilled in hand-to-hand combat and slaying giants. Einherjar often come from warrior cultures, including Ulfen vikings (like the einherji represented in this entry), particularly fierce pirates from the Shackles, and even Osirian conquerors. They can be chosen from wherever war and might hold sway; many deities who hold the call of battle and the pursuit of physical power more sacred than concepts of good and evil might count einherjar warriors and valkyrie choosers of the slain among their chosen servitors. For example, [Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=7), [Besmara](/Deities.aspx?ID=32), and [Sekhmet](/Deities.aspx?ID=58) have all elevated fallen worshippers as einherjar. Einherjar dedicated to different deities often wield weapons or possess varying cosmetic appearances based on their deity's preferred weapon and their place of death; however, they're universally stalwart, implacable, and efficiently deadly. Einherjar with two-handed weapons or who arise from other backgrounds often have different abilities in place of Jotun Slayer and Instant Repair. **[Recall Knowledge - Monitor](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Einherji](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1141) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 89 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Hallit](/Languages.aspx?ID=37), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +21 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +3 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)_, _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31)_, [standard-grade darkwood shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=312) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +18 **Will** +17 (+21 vs. fear) **HP** 175 **Resistances** piercing 10 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)**
**[Shield Block](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=32)** --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** longsword +24 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d8+13 slashing **Melee** fist +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+13 bludgeoning **Melee** dagger +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d4+13 piercing **Ranged** dagger +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d4+13 piercing **Champion Devotion Spells** DC 29, 2 Focus Points - **1st** [Weapon Surge](/Spells.aspx?ID=469) - **4th** [Word of Freedom](/Spells.aspx?ID=470) **Challenge Foe** The einherji challenges one creature they can see to single combat, attempting to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) that target. This target remains the einherji's foe until it's defeated, it flees, or the encounter ends. The einherji gains a circumstance bonus to damage equal to their number of weapon damage dice against their designated foe but takes an equivalent circumstance penalty to damage against any other creature. If the einherji is defeated by their challenged foe, the shame causes them to lose use of their champion devotion spells for 1 week or until they challenge the same foe again and emerge victorious, whichever comes first. **Jotun Slayer** The einherji has a +4 circumstance bonus to damage rolls made against [giants](/Traits.aspx?ID=79) and creatures that are at least two sizes larger than the einherji. **Instant Repair** The einherji [Repairs](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) their shield. They can't use this ability if the shield is completely destroyed.
The Final Battle Regardless of the deity they serve, all einherjar follow certain beliefs and mythologies. They assert that, in the final days of existence, [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) will judge the last soul and spark the beginning of a new existence. Einherjar believe they will be among the last souls left prior to this event, fighting alongside their gods against the forces of entropy. To einherjar, victory in this war—defined as thorough mutual destruction—is the only way to ensure a proper transition into the new existence.
name: Einherji

Air wisps are floating spheres of cloud and storm, perpetually humming a light, whispery tone. They're playful and capricious with great curiosity toward strangers. **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 14 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 9 [Air Wisp](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1142) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 90 **Perception** +6; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Plane of Air Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +1 **Int** +0 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +3 **Ref** +9 **Will** +4 **HP** 10 **Immunities** bleed, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resonance** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)) 30 feet. All wisps vibrate at a frequency attuned to their element, resonating with and empowering all creatures and effects sharing that trait. A creature in the area gains a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls for effects with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait; a creature with the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) and air traits gains this bonus to all attack and damage rolls.
**Accord Essence** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5)) **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet that benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour is targeted by an attack; **Effect** The wisp detonates itself in a small elemental explosion that gives temporary Hit Points equal to half the wisp's current Hit Points to allies within 30 feet that have benefited from the wisp's resonance in the last hour. These temporary Hit Points last 1 hour.
A wisp that uses this reaction is permanently destroyed, and it can be restored only by a [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell or similarly powerful effect. If an ability would prevent the wisp's destruction (for instance, if the wisp is summoned and would merely be dismissed), Accord Essence has no effect. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** tendril +7 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d4 bludgeoning **In Concert** When an air wisp rolls a critical failure on a check to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=75), they get a failure instead, and when they roll a success, they get a critical success instead.
name: Air Wisp

These temporary Hit Points last 1 hour.
A wisp that uses this reaction is permanently destroyed, and it can be restored only by a [_wish_](/Spells.aspx?ID=377) spell or similarly powerful effect.
