<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Globster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1173)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The tide washes ashore all manner of detritus, from harmless seaweed and shells to the rotting corpses of massive aquatic creatures. The globster is often mistaken for such, and this assumption isn't entirely incorrect—these mindless oozing masses are composed of decaying sea creatures, half-digested and merged into a revolting, reeking heap of blubbery sludge.

 Though mindless, globsters are predators that seek out living quarry. They often huddle on the seafloor, where their own fetid mass attracts scavengers who swiftly become the ooze's next meal. When the tides wash these monsters ashore, they simply shift to hunting land-bound prey. Coastal communities usually notice the smell of a washed-up globster long before they see it. Those sent to investigate often mistake a globster for the carcass of a beached whale before getting too close and discovering the presumed corpse is very much alive and hungry.

 Sages once believed globsters were undead, undulating wads of rotting flesh driven to feed, but though mindless, they are very much alive. They are attracted to waterside refuse dumps and floating garbage scows and dimly aware enough to congregate where food is plentiful.

 Globsters consume living creatures but digest only a portion of them. The undigested dross accumulates within the globster as it becomes more and more bloated. They carry this fetid mass within their squelching bodies until instinct or injury provokes them to vomit forth a new globster to help devour everything nearby. A globster with enough dross to create a new globster automatically does so as a free action triggered upon taking damage. Treat any encounter with such a globster as though it were against two level 5 creatures, instead of just one. As far as scholars know, this is the only way these creatures can create more of their kind.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Globster.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Globster](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1173)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="N" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Aquatic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=168" />
<trait label="Ooze" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=122" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 115

**Perception** +9

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +15

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** -5

**Con** +5

**Int** -5

**Wis** +0

**Cha** -5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 12 

**Fort** +16 

**Ref** +6 

**Will** +9 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 170

</row>

**Immunities**
critical hits, [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Weaknesses**
electricity 10

**Stench** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246)) 30 feet. A creature entering the aura or starting its turn in the area must succeed at a DC 19 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (plus [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for as long as it's sickened on a critical failure). While within the aura, affected creatures take a –2 circumstance penalty to saves against [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) and to recover from the sickened condition. A creature that succeeds at its save is temporarily immune to all globsters' stenches for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 15 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tendril +15,
**Damage** 2d8+6 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) and nauseating slap

**[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10)** <actions string="Single Action" />  1d8+6, DC 22

**Nauseating Slap**  ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) A living creature struck by a globster's tendril must attempt a DC 19 Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature becomes [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). If the creature is already sickened, the condition value increases by 1, to a maximum of sickened 4. Once a creature succeeds at its saving throw, it is temporarily immune for 24 hours.

**Saturated**   A globster can survive for 1 hour out of the water, after which it risks [drowning and suffocation](/Rules.aspx?ID=468).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="">Fetid Decomposition</title>

When a globster is killed, its body decays into a mass of goo within 24 hours. However, its stench aura can persist long past its destruction, often lasting for 1d10 days, centered on the globster's position at the time of its death. 
</aside>
name: Globster

markdown:
Fossil golems are typically made from the fossilized bones of dinosaurs and other ancient megafauna, though some crafters stretch the definition and instead use magically petrified bones of dragons or even giants. Not bound to any specific model, fossil golems are crafted to be as terrifying as possible instead of models of anatomic accuracy. As such, most fossil golems are nightmarish saurian amalgams whose massive arms end with the complete skulls of apex predators like tyrannosauruses, their bite enhanced to petrify flesh. The enchantments that animate fossil golems grant them a form of modular flexibility, letting them trade mobility for extended reach through the rapid rearrangement of their limbs. This also lets them appear like a jumbled mass of bones at rest, making them likely to be mistaken for decor rather than guardians. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Fossil Golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1174) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 116 **Perception** +20; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +20 **Will** +18 **HP** 195 **Immunities** acid, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), magic (see below), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374) or bludgeoning) **Golem Antimagic** harmed by [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) and [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) (5d10, 2d8 from areas and persistent damage); healed by [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) (area 2d8 HP); slowed by [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)
**Vulnerable to Stone to Flesh** A [_stone to flesh_](/Spells.aspx?ID=311) spell negates the golem's golem antimagic and its resistance to physical damage for 1 round. A [_flesh to stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=123) spell reverses this effect immediately. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** jaws +26 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+13 piercing plus fossilization **Fossilization** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The first time each round a creature takes damage from the fossil golem's jaws, the target must attempt a DC 32 Fortitude save. If it fails and has not already been slowed by this ability, it becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 minute. If the creature was already slowed by this ability, a failed save causes it to be [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30) permanently. **Reassemble** The fossil golem reorganizes its bones, increasing its reach to 25 feet and reducing its Speed to 15 feet. It can revert to its original form by taking this action again.
Digging is Optional Intact fossilized bones are difficult components to scrounge, much less whole skeletons. An alternate way to obtain them is to hire expert hunters or adventurers to seek out live dinosaurs, bring back the bones, and use magic to turn them to stone. Of course, some purists prefer genuine fossilized bones and are willing to pay the price for their acquisition. Either way, opportunities await enterprising souls with the proper tools and several [_bags of holding_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=249).
name: Fossil Golem

markdown:
Mithral golems are gorgeously crafted, silvery humanoids made from colossal amounts of the purest [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275). While massive in construction, they possess amazing speed and grace through literal fluidity. Unpredictable and flexible, they can melt into liquid form and reform into a solid shape in mere seconds, allowing them to shape their limbs into sharp spikes or heavy fists as needed. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Mithral Golem](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1175) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 117 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +30, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +33 **Str** +9 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 40 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +30 **Will** +24 **HP** 220 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=9), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), magic (see below), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) **Evasion** When the mithral golem rolls a success on a Reflex save, it gets a critical success instead.
**Golem Antimagic** harmed by [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) and [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) (8d10, 2d10 from areas and persistent damage); healed by [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157) (area 2d10 HP); slowed by [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)
**Swift Steps** A mithral golem is permanently [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32), and it can use the extra action only to Step or Stride.
**Vulnerable to Slow** A mithral golem is not healed by a [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=289) spell, and such spells affect them normally. A [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=147) spell reverses the effects of a _slow_ spell immediately. --- **Speed** 50 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** fist +31 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+12 bludgeoning **Melee** spike +31 ([deadly 2d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+12 piercing **Liquefy** The mithral golem becomes fully liquid and amorphous. It gains immunity to precision damage, but it takes double damage from [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) and [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) magic (see golem antimagic). The only actions it can use while Liquefied are [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions, Spike Storm, and spike Strikes. It can resume its normal form by taking this action again. **Spike Storm** The mithral golem makes a spike Strike against each target within its reach. These attacks count toward the mithral golem's multiple attack penalty, but its multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until after all the attacks.
Creative Fluidity The mithral golem's fluid form offers endless possibilities for inventive owners looking to deploy these constructs within their trap-filled domains. The golem can be instructed to remain in its liquid phase until a specific triggering event, appearing as a pool or font filled with quicksilver, or covering columns or statues. Truly fiendish minds might store them in hidden reservoirs hooked to pumps and nozzles, ready to spray a dangerous foe onto unsuspecting adventurers
name: Mithral Golem

markdown:
Green men are ancient, enigmatic lesser deities of the primeval forests, living embodiments of nature and plantkind. When a forest or other woodland terrain generates enough nature spirits, beings of the same sort of vital essence that embodies [leshy](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=67) or answers the call of a commune with nature, they naturally coalesce together and apotheosize into a green man. Green men aren't concerned with all the multifarious processes of nature like [Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=8) or many other nature deities. Instead, they focus nearly all their attention on the plants of their home, only concerning themselves with animals, minerals, and the like insomuch as they affect the plants. Despite their name, green men aren't necessarily male; as creatures of pure natural power, to many of them, the concept of gender holds no meaning, and to those that do, they can be of any gender. Most green men are neutral and tend to ignore “animals,” which to them include sapient creatures such as [humans](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6). However, good and evil green men do exist. These individuals are far more likely to attempt to spread their influence far and wide, either for good or ill. Good green men provide succor to all that come within their home, not only to plants, providing wisdom like a nurturing parent. Evil green men, however, allow rare and dangerous plants to thrive in their domains by spreading fear and devastation to all those who might threaten plant life, though they might keep a few animals around to hunt for sport. **[Recall Knowledge - Plant](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 53 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 51 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 [Green Man](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1176) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 118 **Perception** +42; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), plantsense 60 feet **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Druidic](/Languages.aspx?ID=23), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10); green tongue **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +39, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +42, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +40, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +40, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +48, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +41, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +44 **Str** +12 **Dex** +9 **Con** +11 **Int** +7 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +8 **Green Tongue** A green man can communicate with plants, with the effects of [_speak with plants_](/Spells.aspx?ID=294), and can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on plants and [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) things from plants.
**Plantsense** A green man can sense life force via plants. This allows them to observe a living or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature's vital essence within 60 feet of the green man, but they can also use this precise sense to observe any living or undead creature within 60 feet of any plant matter within 120 feet of the green man. This allows the green man to see living things through solid plant matter, as well as seeing through other barriers if there are plants on the other side. --- **AC** 51 **Fort** +43 **Ref** +39 **Will** +42 **HP** 525 **Resistances** bludgeoning 20, piercing 20 **Weaknesses** axe vulnerability, fire 20 **Axe Vulnerability** A green man takes 20 additional damage from [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1).
**Green Caress** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=125), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) 60 feet. Living creatures in the area other than [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=125) slowly transform into non-creature plants. The green man can exclude creatures from this effect, but they must be aware of a creature's presence and location to do so. A non-plant creature in the area must attempt a DC 45 Fortitude save immediately before the start of its turn.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected, or if it is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) by green caress, it reduces its slowed value by 2.
**Success** The creature is unaffected, or if it is slowed by green caress, it reduces its slowed value by 1.
**Failure** The creature becomes slowed 1, or if it was already slowed by green caress, increases the slowed value by 1, as their body transforms more and more into a non-creature plant. If the creature ever becomes slowed to the point they have no actions left for their turn, they become an inanimate plant, a condition that can only be reversed by [_primal phenomenon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=231) or similarly powerful magic.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature becomes slowed 2 (or increases the condition value by 2).
**Root In Place** **Trigger** A creature within the green man's reach uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) action or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The green man lashes out to hold the foe in place. The green man makes a vine Strike against the triggering creature. If the attack hits, the green man disrupts the action. If the creature was [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) when its action was disrupted, it falls. --- **Speed** 40 feet, climb 40 feet **Melee** vine +46 ([deadly 3d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d10+27 bludgeoning plus absorb magic and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Ranged** thorn +43 ([fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=178), [range increment 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254)), **Damage** 4d8+27 piercing plus embed **Primal Innate Spells** DC 48 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) - **8th** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128) - **9th** [Energy Aegis](/Spells.aspx?ID=100) - **10th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) (×3), [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=248) (×3), [Tree Stride](/Spells.aspx?ID=343) (at will), [True Seeing](/Spells.aspx?ID=344), [Primal Phenomenon](/Spells.aspx?ID=231) (×3) **Rituals** DC 48, green rituals (see below) - **4th** [Plant Growth](/Rituals.aspx?ID=18) - **6th** [Awaken Animal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3), [Commune with Nature](/Rituals.aspx?ID=7), [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9) **Absorb Magic** The green man's vines leach away magic and transform it into life essence for the green man. On a successful vine Strike, the green man attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) one spell active on the target (typically one vexing the green man, or determined randomly if they aren't aware of specific effects), with a counteract level of 10 and a modifier of +38. If the effect is counteracted, the green man gains 30 temporary Hit Points that last for 10 minutes. **Embed** The green man's thorns embed themselves into any creature they damage, taking root into the ground. A target damaged by a thorn has its Speeds halved, and it can't Step, [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), or otherwise leave the ground until the thorn is removed. Removing a thorn requires 3 Interact actions, which don't have to be consecutive. If the creature performing the final action doesn't succeed at a DC 45 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check as part of that action, the target takes 10d6 damage upon the thorn's removal. **Focus Vines** The green man focuses all their vines against a single vexing foe, making a single vine Strike. On a success, the target takes 5d10 additional damage and is affected by Absorb Magic three times. Even on a failure, the target takes the normal effects of a hit with a vine Strike, but on a critical failure, the vines miss completely. **Green Grab** A green man can use their [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) action against a creature of any size. **Green Rituals** A green man can perform all their rituals without secondary casters, relying on their own primal ties to the vital essence in spirits of nature. A green man's [_awaken animal_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3) and [_primal call_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19) rituals work on plants instead of their usual range of choices. Most green men also know the ritual to create various types of [leshys](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=14) and possibly even magic allowing the creation of [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8) or more powerful plant creatures. **Vine Forest** The green man lashes out with all six vines to attack many opponents. They make up to six vine Strikes, each against a different target; this counts as one attack for their multiple attack penalty, increasing only after all the attacks are made.
Leshy and Green Men Green men are sometimes called “leshy kings” or even just “leshys” in ancient manuscripts. Druids consider the first green men to be the original source of the rituals for creating the less-deific [leshys](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=14), and possibly even the [arboreals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=8) and other intelligent plants. The affinity between leshys and green men is mutual. Leshys are the most likely ancestry to [worship green man](/Deities.aspx?ID=221), and green men are particularly fond of their less-powerful kin
Worshipping Green Men Individual [green men](/Deities.aspx?ID=221) are lesser deities, capable of granting spells to those who worship them. Green men typically only allow intelligent plants—such as leshys—to be their clerics. If another creature proves to be a friend of plants, after a thorough personal vetting, a green man wholeheartedly accepts this strange fleshy worshipper into the fold. While individual green men have different edicts and anathema befitting their personalities, and some neutral good or neutral evil green men might have further changes, the following is a baseline most worshippers of green men follow.
name: Green Man

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Grimple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1177)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Even more than most gremlins, grimples resent the trappings of civilization: inn common rooms with their rowdy singalongs, livery yards with their whinnying horses, church steeples with their clanging bells. Grimples live to spoil these conventions, dropping tavern signs on patrons, urinating in rain barrels, and opening stable doors. When all else fails, they literally vomit their disdain on passersby.

 Grimples resemble humanoid, mange-ridden opossums, with boar-like tusks that aid them in rooting through garbage heaps for food. They are agile climbers who glide from eave to eave on the loose flaps of skin between their limbs. Savvy gremlin-hunters know to look for the skin flakes and fur grimples shed from their parasite-infested hides.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Gremlin_Grimple.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Grimple](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1177)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
<trait label="Gremlin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=221" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 120

**Perception** +6; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11)

**Skills**
[Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +2, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +4, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +3

**Int** +1

**Wis** +2

**Cha** -2
</row>

**Items**
satchel with 5 rocks

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +4 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 9

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 2

**Gremlin Lice** Whenever a living creature touches or is touched by a grimple (including via a successful unarmed melee Strike), it must succeed at a DC 13 Reflex save or become infested by gremlin lice. While infested, the targeted creature is distracted by the itching sensation and is [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37), though it can use an Interact action to scratch at the itching lice to suppress the stupefied condition from the lice for 1d4 rounds. The infestation ends after 24 hours or until the creature is submerged in water or exposed to a [severe cold environment](/Rules.aspx?ID=642), whichever comes first.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, climb 20 feet, fly 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
rock +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 16
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229)
- **1st**
[Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140)

**Putrid Vomit** <actions string="Single Action" />  The grimple spews a 30-foot line of vomit. Each creature in the line must succeed at a DC 16 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The grimple can't use Putrid Vomit again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Grimple Allies</title>

[Vexgits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=688) and grimples sometimes team up to cause ruin. Squatting in abandoned clock or bell towers, insectile vexgits use cranky grimples to lure victims into elaborate death traps. Left to their own devices, grimples bully [mitflits](/Monsters.aspx?ID=245) or train [giant rats](/Monsters.aspx?ID=346) and [spider swarms](/Monsters.aspx?ID=385) to do their bidding.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-58" />
name: Grimple

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Haniver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1178)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hanivers are the most benign type of gremlin—capricious fey who skim the waves on rubbery fin-wings. Though they don't actively sabotage their environment, sailors bemoan these gremlins' grasping fingers. Hanivers' incessant curiosity compels them to examine any object that draws their attention. Such treasures might include an overturned fruit basket, a sack of coins, or a shark's gleaming teeth (often still in the shark's mouth, to the fey's regret). Should hanivers like what they find, they steal it, making them a bane to fishers and dockworkers everywhere.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 13

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 8
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature -1">[Haniver](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1178)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
<trait label="Gremlin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=221" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 120

**Perception** +5; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +4, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +3, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +5, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +5

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +3

**Con** +2

**Int** -1

**Wis** +1

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 15 

**Fort** +4 

**Ref** +7 

**Will** +3 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 9

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 2

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 10 feet, fly 20 feet, swim 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 13
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229)
- **1st**
[Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (at will)

**Rearrange Possessions** <actions string="Single Action or Two Actions" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The haniver attempts to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) a small object off a target's person. If they succeed, they also rifle through and rearrange the contents of the target's pockets, pouches, and other containers. The next time the target attempts to draw a weapon or retrieve a worn item, doing so requires two Interact actions instead of one. The haniver can Steal an object that's closely guarded using this action without the –5 penalty, though not objects that would be extremely noticeable or time-consuming to remove. They can spend 2 actions instead of 1 to use this ability to Steal from a creature in combat or otherwise on guard.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Mari Haniver</title>

Sailors sometimes affix a “Mari Haniver” to their ship's prow. Made from a ray's dried carcass, this clever bit of taxidermy resembles a dead haniver enough to scare gremlins away. But should a haniver wise up to the ruse, it will stop at nothing to torment its would-be deceivers. 
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-58" />
name: Haniver

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Fuath](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1179)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Despite their small size, fuaths are shipwreckers incarnate. In the dark of night, these gremlins sever ratlines, slash sails, smash sextants, and soil provisions. Once the ship founders, the fuaths return to indulge their craving for land-raised flesh. While fuaths prefer to take their prey asleep, they save a terrible doom for sailors who attack them, surrounding the mariners' faces in magically congealed water to drown them where they stand.

 Constantly dripping with water, fuaths have seahorse-like faces, seaweed-green fur over yellow skin, and wicked lobster claws for hands. Lacking the Undercommon tongue, they have trouble relating to other gremlins aside from [hanivers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1178), but revere [sea hags](/Monsters.aspx?ID=254) and evil aquatic fey.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 15

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Gremlin_Fuath.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 1">[Fuath](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1179)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Tiny" />
<trait label="Amphibious" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=207" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
<trait label="Gremlin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=221" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 121

**Perception** +8; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Aquan](/Languages.aspx?ID=14)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +4, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +6, [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +7

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +1

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +1

**Wis** +3

**Cha** -1
</row>

**Items**
[darts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68) (6)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 17 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +9 

**Will** +6 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 18

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 2, fire 2

**Vulnerable to Sunlight** A fuath becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) (or increases its drained condition by 1) after every consecutive hour they're exposed to sunlight. Being submerged in more than a foot of water prevents the sunlight from harming the fuath.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 20 feet, swim 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 1d6+1 slashing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
dart +9 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [range increment 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d4+1 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 17
- **Cantrips (1st)**
[Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229)
- **1st**
[Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288)

**Viscous Choke** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The fuath surrounds the head of one air-breathing creature within 30 feet in a magical film of viscous water for 1 minute. The target must succeed at a DC 17 Reflex save or begin to choke and must hold their breath to avoid [drowning](/Rules.aspx?ID=468). The film can be temporarily wiped away with a total of 3 Interact actions by the choking creature or creatures adjacent to it, allowing a new Reflex save with a +2 circumstance bonus to end the effect. (These actions don't need to be consecutive or made by the same creature.)

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Fuath Guardians</title>

Lone fuaths sometimes appoint themselves guardians of nature, protecting spawning grounds from overfishing or preventing careless cutting of peat bogs. Most, however, are unrepentant saboteurs.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-58" />
name: Fuath

The first grioths encountered on a new world are scouts. Typically traveling the vastness of space via one-way portals from their home worlds, grioth scouts never expect to see their homes again, as grioth leadership ensures true devotion to the colonization effort by stranding them on new worlds. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 17 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12 [Grioth Scout](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1180) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 122 **Perception** +7; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), echolocation (precise) 20 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Grioth](/Languages.aspx?ID=65); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +7 **Str** +0 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Echolocation** A grioth can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range. **Items** [voidglass kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56) --- **AC** 17 **Fort** +5 **Ref** +9 **Will** +7 **HP** 16 **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** fire 3 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)**
**No Breath** A grioth doesn't breathe except to speak and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** kukri +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d6 slashing **Melee** jaws +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d4 piercing plus grioth venom **Occult Innate Spells** DC 16, attack +8 - **Cantrips (1st)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **1st** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) **Grioth Venom** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 17; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (1 round); **Stage 2** frightened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** frightened 3 (1 round) **Shock Mind** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The grioth scout makes a Strike with a voidglass weapon. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 1d6 mental damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 17 Will save (this has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait) or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round.

markdown:
Grioth cultists represent the lowest tier of their remorseless cult—worshippers of the [Elder Mythos](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13) tasked with leading scouting missions into new worlds. Though ranked above scouts in the chain of command, they occupy a lowly place in the overall religious hierarchy. Though whispered rumors describe the legendary rituals needed to wrench worlds from their suns, these cultists don't learn this potent magic. Such secrets are left to grioth high priests, who are always the last to arrive in a colonized world. Most grioths worship the Outer God [Nyarlathotep](/Deities.aspx?ID=164) in one of his many incarnations, although some worship other entities of the Elder Mythos such as [Azathoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=161), [Nhimbaloth](/Deities.aspx?ID=163), or [Yog-Sothoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=166). In the highly rare cases where a grioth is cut off from their society and given the opportunity to explore other religions, they still lean into faiths associated with the stars or the night, such as [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5) or [Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=20). However, these grioths are loathed by others of their own kind, who view them as dangerous heretics. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Grioth Cultist](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1181) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 123 **Perception** +10; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), echolocation (precise) 20 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Grioth](/Languages.aspx?ID=65); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 30 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +9, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +10 **Str** +0 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Echolocation** A grioth can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range **Items** [voidglass kukri](/Weapons.aspx?ID=56) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +7 **Ref** +10 **Will** +10 **HP** 40 **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** fire 5 **[Light Blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=22)**
**No Breath** A grioth doesn't breathe except to speak and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)). --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** kukri +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d6+2 slashing **Melee** jaws +10 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d8+2 piercing plus grioth venom **Divine Prepared Spells** DC 20, attack +12 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=84), [Forbidding Ward](/Spells.aspx?ID=126), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=190), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=307) - **1st** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) - **2nd** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 19, attack +11 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=66), [Mage Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=177), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=334) - **2nd** [Phantom Pain](/Spells.aspx?ID=220) **Grioth Venom** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)) **Saving Throw** Fortitude DC 20; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (1 round); **Stage 2** frightened 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** frightened 3 (1 round) **Shock Mind** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The grioth scout makes a Strike with a voidglass weapon. If the Strike hits, it deals an additional 2d6 mental damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 20 Will save (this has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait) or become [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round. **Invoke Haunter of the Dark** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The grioth cultist waves a hand in a complex pattern to invoke dark powers, dealing 3d8 mental damage. Each non-grioth creature within 20 feet must attempt a DC 20 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and becomes stupefied 1 for 1 minute.
name: Grioth Cultist

markdown:
Stone lion cubs are curious, playful, and occasionally accidental mischief makers. Despite their still developing abilities, they eagerly hone their skills with make-believe missions. Many hope that they'll one day become responsible guardians like their parents. Smaller in stature and ferocity, stone lion cubs sometimes try to guard places of smaller importance, often including facsimiles of the same kinds of structures their parents attend. They're less than dependable as guardians, as they have the attention span and rambunctious nature of children. Their parents often need to call them to heel, ordering them to stay at the parent's side as parent and child both stay motionless for the rising day. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Stone Lion Cub](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1182) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 124 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 60 feet **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +7, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Meteorology Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +7, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +8 **Str** +3 **Dex** +3 **Con** +0 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Anchored Soul** The cub is mystically bonded to its bonded vessel and must remain within 1 mile of it. Some might be further restricted to the location it guards. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +6 **Ref** +7 **Will** +10 +1 status to all saves vs. evil **HP** 28 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Bonded Vessel** The condition of a stone cub's vessel dictates the cub's maximum Hit Point value. Undamaged, the vessel is an object with 28 Hit Points (BT 14). When the cub is in spirit form, damaging it doesn't hurt the vessel, but damaging the vessel deals an equal amount of damage to the cub. When the cub Inhabits its Vessel, they're a single target, and damage reduces the Hit Points of both the cub and the vessel. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), the cub can still fight normally while inhabiting it and suffers no ill effect, but if the vessel is ever destroyed, the cub is instantly slain and can't reconstitute.
**Reconstitution** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the cub reaches 0 Hit Points, its spirit dissipates. If its bonded vessel is intact, the cub re-forms in this vessel after 2d4 days, fully healed. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), it must first be [Repaired](/Actions.aspx?ID=42), after which the cub reforms in 3d4 days. --- **Speed** fly 25 feet **Divine Innate Spells** DC 17 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; evil only) **Inhabit Vessel** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The cub touches and melds with its bonded vessel, bringing the statue to life. It can cease Inhabiting its Vessel by spending a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. While Inhabiting the Vessel, it loses its fly Speed and gains **Immunities** [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188); **Resistances** physical 3 (except bludgeoning); **Speed** 20 feet; and the following Strike.
  • **Melee** jaws +10 ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d6+5 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)
**Spirit Body** When not Inhabiting its Vessel, the cub is [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) and gains resistance 3 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage and damage from Strikes with the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune; double resistance against non-magical).
Bleeding Omen Legends say that stone lions can predict incoming storms or floods. If a stone lion's eyes, nose, or ears bleed, preparations should be made. Lone survivors of these disasters claim those who disparage such omens as superstition often don't live to see the next dawn.
name: Stone Lion Cub

Stone lions are a common sight outside of banks, temples, palaces, and even private residences. The fierce expressions affixed on their faces remind passersby to stay on their best behavior. Stone lions remain faithful guardians of a site, regardless of whether it falls into disrepair. Even if people abandon a place, the lion remains dutiful until its statue is too worn or broken to inhabit any longer. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Stone Lion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1183) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 125 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Celestial](/Languages.aspx?ID=16), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 60 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +8, [Meteorology Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +11, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 **Anchored Soul** The stone lion is mystically bonded to its bonded vessel and must remain within 1 mile of it. Some might be further restricted to the location it guards. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +9 **Will** +13 +1 status to all saves vs. evil **HP** 50 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=30), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) **Bonded Vessel** The condition of a stone lion's vessel dictates the lion's maximum Hit Point value. Undamaged, the vessel is an object with 50 Hit Points (BT 25). When the stone lion is in spirit form, damaging it doesn't hurt the vessel, but damaging the vessel deals an equal amount of damage to the stone lion. When the stone lion Inhabits its Vessel, they're a single target, and damage reduces the Hit Points of both the stone lion and the vessel. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), the stone lion can still fight normally while inhabiting it and suffers no ill effect, but if the vessel is ever destroyed, the stone lion is instantly slain and can't reconstitute.
**Reconstitution** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the stone lion reaches 0 Hit Points, its spirit dissipates. If its bonded vessel is intact, the stone lion re-forms in this vessel after 2d4 days, fully healed. If the vessel is [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2), it must first be [Repaired](/Actions.aspx?ID=42), after which the stone lion reforms in 3d4 days. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Divine Innate Spells** DC 21 - **1st** [Detect Alignment](/Spells.aspx?ID=65) (at will; evil only) - **3rd** [Dream Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=90) **Ferocious Roar** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The lion makes a terrifying roar that deals 2d8 sonic damage (DC 23 basic Fortitude save) to each creature in a 20-foot cone. Creatures that fail this save become [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). **Inhabit Vessel** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The lion touches and melds with its bonded vessel, bringing the statue to life. It can cease Inhabiting its Vessel by spending a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait. While Inhabiting the Vessel, it loses its fly Speed and gains **Immunities** [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188);**Resistances** physical 5 (except bludgeoning); **Speed** 30 feet; and the following Strikes.
  • **Melee** [one-action] jaws +14, **Damage** 2d6+7 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)
  • **Ranged** [one-action] stone ball +13 ([range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning
**Spirit Body** When not Inhabiting its Vessel, the stone lion is [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) and gains resistance 5 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) damage and damage from Strikes with the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune; double resistance against non-magical).
Bleeding Omen Legends say that stone lions can predict incoming storms or floods. If a stone lion's eyes, nose, or ears bleed, preparations should be made. Lone survivors of these disasters claim those who disparage such omens as superstition often don't live to see the next dawn.

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Guecubu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1184)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Sometimes an individual performs acts of horror so unspeakable that the earth they walk on becomes imprinted by their evil. With such villains, even slaying them and scattering their bones can't rid the world of their vile presence. Their souls rise again as guecubus, gruesome skeletons made of scorched bones, rotting flesh, and befouled earth. Guecubus roams the land seeking those they deem responsible for their death.

 A guecubu remembers what was done to them, and the memories of their supposed mistreatment fuel their quest for revenge. Guecubus are well-equipped to exact such vengeance. Along with monstrous power and the ability to move through earth, they retain all the cunning they had in life. These creatures are known to kill their enemies one by one, leaving no trace of their presence behind save pebbles and dust.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Guecubu.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 8">[Guecubu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1184)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="CE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Undead" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=160" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 126

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +4

**Con** +3

**Int** +4

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +3
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110
([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42))

</row>

**Immunities**
[death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38)

**Resistances**
electricity 10, physical 10 (except bludgeoning)

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 15 feet; earth glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus wrathful misfortune

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 26
- **4th**
[Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276) (at will)

**Break Ground** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The guecubu stomps, and the ground breaks and ripples in a 30-foot emanation, erupting in razor-sharp, crisscrossing spikes of rock that deal 3d8 piercing damage to creatures in the area (DC 26 basic Reflex save). The area becomes difficult terrain and hazardous terrain, with each square dealing 3 piercing damage to a creature that moves through it. A guecubu is immune to these effects. Ground under the effects of a [_consecrate_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8) ritual or a [_circle of protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=38) spell tuned against evil or chaos can't be affected by this ability.

**Earth Glide**   The guecubu can Burrow through any earthen matter, including rock. When it does so, the guecubu moves at its full burrow Speed, leaving no tunnels or signs of its passing.

**Shove into Stone**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) When the guecubu successfully Shoves a creature into a stone barrier, the target must succeed at a DC 26 Reflex save or become merged with the barrier, with the effects of [_meld into stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=188) until the target [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (DC 26).

**Wrathful Misfortune**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=11), [Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61)) A creature damaged by a guecubu must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or become cursed with consummate bad luck. The creature becomes [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3). Each time that creature rolls initiative, on their next roll they must roll twice and use the worse result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">You Again?</title>

A guecubu is one of the clearest manifestations of selfish evil found in the world. They believe they were raised to make their enemies pay for their demise, completely in denial to their own role in those events. Bereft of the henchmen and power they commanded while alive, the guecubu gleefully uses their new powers on those they hate. Many adventurers realize at some point that the guecubu pursuing them is a former foe they believed dispatched.
</aside>
name: Guecubu

markdown:
The bizarre hadrinnexes resemble defensive systems more than living creatures. They evolve at a rapid rate, but only to specific attacks against them, which suggests they were created through advanced technology or magic. The husk surrounding a hadrinnex—flecks of metal suspended in a malleable organic carapace—reshapes in response to harm. So too can a glowing gland within the creature's thorax, which collects energy and restructures the creature's biology to protect it from that energy. This organ is fragile and ruptures soon after a hadrinnex is killed. Hadrinnexes have only a rudimentary intellect and usually follow more intelligent aberrations. As bodyguards or peons, they perform simple tasks diligently. Though poor at problem-solving and improvisation, hadrinnexes' dependability and adaptive physiology make them ideal for dangerous tasks in hazardous environments. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Hadrinnex](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1185) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 127 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) (touch) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +11 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** -3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +14 **Will** +17 **HP** 118 **Resistances** energy 15, physical 15 (see Rapid Evolution) **Rapid Evolution** ([morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=113), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) **Trigger** The hadrinnex takes damage of a physical or energy damage type (bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing for physical; acid, cold, electricity, fire, force, negative, or sonic for energy) **Effect** The hadrinnex reconfigures its husk (if triggered by physical damage) or its energy gland (if triggered by energy damage). Any reconfiguration applies to the triggering damage and lasts until the next time the hadrinnex uses Rapid Evolution.
  • **Energy Gland** Reconfiguring the energy gland changes both the hadrinnex's energy damage resistance and the damage of its energy ray to that type. By default, the energy gland is configured to sonic.
  • **Husk** The hadrinnex's physical damage resistance and the damage of its weapon arm Strikes change to the triggering type. Weapon arm Strikes gain an additional trait depending on the current damage type: bludgeoning adds [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193), piercing adds [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), and slashing adds [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=194). By default the husk is configured to bludgeoning.
 --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** weapon arm +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+9 physical (see Rapid Evolution) **Ranged** energy ray +18 ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [range 120 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 5d6 energy (see Rapid Evolution) **Extend Limbs** The hadrinnex makes two weapon arm Strikes, each targeting a different creature. The hadrinnex's reach increases to 20 feet for these Strikes. **Vent Energy** ([Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) The hadrinnex purges the energy in its energy gland for an external discharge. It either blasts the energy to deal 7d6 energy damage to creatures in a 30-foot cone (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save), or directs the energy to its weapon arms, making its weapon arm Strikes deal an extra 2d6 energy damage for 1 minute. Either one expends the damage type stored in the hadrinnex's energy gland, as described below.
After the energy is vented, the energy gland goes dormant. The hadrinnex loses its energy resistance and can't use energy ray until it uses Rapid Evolution to reconfigure its energy gland again. Directing energy to its weapon arms again removes any previous energy boost to its weapon arm.
Hibernation Pods Hadrinnexes can be found hibernating in remote areas, their husks shaped into ovoid pods. These pods are usually centered in craters, suggesting the hadrinnex fell from above. Leaving hibernation takes 2 months as it extrudes its limbs and restarts biological processes. This can be accelerated with a daily influx of energy to its energy gland.
name: Hadrinnex

markdown:
Storm hags are the personification of a storm's rage. These crones hate those who huddle together with loved ones in warm, dry shelters while the wind howls and rain lashes the land and sea. Storm hags use their powers to wreak havoc on communities near the coastal caves or hovels where they often live. These hags send their voices on the wind to terrorize mortals before descending upon them with claws and fell magic. More than any others of their kind, they prey on the young, devouring children as much for the pain it causes their parents and caretakers as for the hags' love of young flesh. A storm hag's territory sometimes overlaps with that of [sea hags](/Monsters.aspx?ID=254) and [winter hags](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1187). While this may result in the formation of a coven that controls their combined territories, more often it leads to rivalries. Such feuds only increase the misery of innocent people caught between the rival hags' feuding. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Storm Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1186) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 128 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), stormsight **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Auran](/Languages.aspx?ID=15), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8); voice of the storm **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +13, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +9, [Weather Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +13 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **[Coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=11)** A storm hag adds [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=155), [_lightning storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=701), and [_mariner's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=184) to her coven's spells.
**Stormsight** Wind, precipitation, and clouds don't impair a storm hag's vision; she ignores the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) condition from storms, mist, precipitation, and the like.
**Voice of the Storm** A storm hag can send spoken messages or sounds on the wind to any spot that she has seen and the wind can reach within a 50-mile radius. She can send a quiet whisper or a loud scream, and decides whether it's clearly audible or barely heard above the wind. The message is delivered regardless of whether anyone is present to hear it. The hag can use this ability to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53) creatures that hear her message with dire threats or unnerving whispers of doom. --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +9 **Will** +12 **HP** 95 **Immunities** electricity **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet **Melee** claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d6+7 slashing plus 1d6 electricity **Melee** jaws +15, **Damage** 1d8+7 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Ranged** wind blast +13 ([Air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d10+5 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 22 - **3rd** [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (at will), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=367) **Stormcalling** A storm hag can perform a special [_control weather_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=9) ritual, which requires no secondary casters, to change the weather within 5 miles of her location for 4d12 hours. The primary check is a DC 23 [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check, and she can't get an outcome worse than a failure. The storm hag can create only hurricanes, thunderstorms, and tornadoes, but she can do so regardless of the current season. She can also quell natural weather events but never willingly does so. **Wind Mastery** A storm hag is unaffected by strong winds, natural or magical. Windy conditions are not difficult terrain for her.
name: Storm Hag

markdown:
On long, dark winter nights, folk at the edges of civilization have learned to be wary of old women who come knocking at their door. They look for her tracks in the snow and other telltale signs to make sure she isn't a winter hag who plans to drag them off and feast on their bones. In truth, winter hags are more subtle monsters, entrapping humanoids in schemes that create mistrust and anger within a village before picking off the residents one or two at a time, heightening the fear and anger of those who remain. When her work is done, a winter hag revels in the silence of a frost-rimed village in which no hearth fires are left burning. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Winter Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1187) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 129 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271), snow vision **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +17, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +15, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +14 **Str** +4 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +6 **[Coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=11)** A winter hag adds [_cone of cold_](/Spells.aspx?ID=47), [_solid fog_](/Spells.aspx?ID=290), and [_wall of ice_](/Spells.aspx?ID=364) to her coven's spells.
**Snow Vision** Snow doesn't impair a winter hag's vision; she ignores [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) from snowfall. --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +13 **Will** +16 **HP** 145 **Immunities** cold **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, fire 5 --- **Speed** 25 feet; ice climb 25 feet **Melee** _ice staff_ +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=198)), **Damage** 2d4+7 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold **Melee** claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+7 slashing plus 1d6 cold **Occult Innate Spells** DC 25, attack +17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Chill Touch](/Spells.aspx?ID=35), [Ray of Frost](/Spells.aspx?ID=245) - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=104), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=213) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=125) (at will), [Ice Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=695) - **Constant (4th)** [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215), [See Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) **Craft Ice Staff** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [Downtime](/Traits.aspx?ID=49), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A winter hag can spend 1 day performing a specific ritual to create a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12)_ of black ice. Once per day, she can use the staff to cast [_cone of cold_](/Spells.aspx?ID=47). The staff's magic functions only in the hands of the hag who created it; for anyone else, it is a mundane staff. If the hag dies or if the staff remains in another creature's possession for 24 hours, the staff melts into a puddle of foul-smelling water. **Ice Climb** A winter hag can [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33) at the listed Speed, but only on ice. She ignores difficult terrain from ice and snow, and she doesn't risk falling when crossing ice.
name: Winter Hag

markdown:
Blood hags, also known as soucouyants, infiltrate communities in the guise of young, innocent humanoids. This disguise is more than an illusion, for a blood hag wears the skin of a previous victim to hide her true appearance. During the day, her disguise is almost perfect, especially if the community knows nothing about the person the hag is pretending to be. But after sunset, the creature sheds her skin, hides it in a safe place, and stalks the night to drink the blood that sustains her. Able to travel quickly in the form of a ball of fire and to slip through keyholes or the slightest crack in a door or window, blood hags feed on sleeping victims then return home before morning to don their stolen skin. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Blood Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1188) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 130 **Perception** +17; bloodsense (imprecise) 90 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Abyssal](/Languages.aspx?ID=12), [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Infernal](/Languages.aspx?ID=19), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +15, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +16, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Bloodsense** A blood hag can sense the presence of blood and creatures with blood. She can tell the difference between spilled blood and the blood within a living creature.
**[Coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=11)** A blood hag adds [_death ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=64), [_fiery body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=115), and [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=208) to her coven's spells.
**Borrowed Skin** A blood hag wears a covering of skin stolen from a [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature she has killed, hiding her true form and granting her the effects of a 4th-level [_misdirection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=198), with herself as the primary target and the creature whose skin she is wearing as the secondary target. Spreading coarse salt inside the skin prevents the hag from putting it back on, forcing her to keep her fiery form until she kills another humanoid and spends 1 hour turning it into a new disguise. --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +17 **Will** +17 **HP** 170 **Immunities** bleed **Resistances** fire 10 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** jaws +18, **Damage** 2d12+7 piercing **Ranged** firebolt +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)), **Damage** 2d10+8 fire **Occult Innate Spells** DC 26 - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=34), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=288) (×3) **Assume Fiery Form** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=127), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The blood hag removes her borrowed skin and transforms into a brilliant ball of fire. She becomes amorphous, gains the [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) trait and a fly Speed of 60 feet, becomes immune to fire, and emits light as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60). She loses her melee Strikes and can't Drain Blood, but she deals 3d10 fire damage (DC 26 basic Reflex save) to each creature that touches her, as well as to each creature that succeeds at a melee Strike against her with an unarmed attack or from an adjacent space. If her skin is intact, she can return to her normal form by spending a single action that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait while adjacent to the skin.
The hag can instead Assume Fiery Form as a single action, bursting through her skin in a blast of flames. Doing so destroys her borrowed skin and deals 9d6 fire damage (DC 26 basic Reflex save) in a 20-foot emanation. **Drain Blood** ([Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Requirements** A [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38), or willing creature is within the blood hag's reach; **Effect** The hag sinks her fangs into the creature to drink its blood. This requires a successful [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the victim's Fortitude DC if the victim is grabbed and is automatic for any of the other conditions. The victim becomes [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). The hag regains 15 Hit Points, gaining any excess HP as temporary Hit Points that last for 1 hour. Drinking blood from a creature that's already drained doesn't restore any Hit Points to the hag but increases the victim's drained value by 1, killing the victim when it reaches drained 5.
A victim's drained condition decreases by 1 per week. A blood transfusion, which requires a successful DC 20 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check and sufficient blood or a blood donor, reduces the drained condition by 1 after 10 minutes.
Blood Hag Skin A slain blood hag's skin can be used as a component in dark rituals invoking demonic powers. A hero who knows this usually destroys the skin. Less scrupulous adventurers can sell this prize for a substantial sum (80–120 gp). They may later learn that they have helped the buyer unleash a terrible scourge upon the world—if the buyer doesn't kill them first.
name: Blood Hag

markdown:
Moon hags are powerful soothsayers who dwell in places where souls receive their final judgment. These vile creatures possess knowledge of—and perhaps some power over—the portals between the lands of the living and the dead, and innocent mortals sometimes seek them out for this reason. Moon hags use their knowledge to sow discord and place seeds of doubt in those who unwittingly seek their aid. Beings of great power can intimidate them into sharing the truth of what they know, but the cunning hags always try to hide some important piece of information or word things in such a way that anyone acting on their divinations still find their plans falling into ruin. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 29 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Moon Hag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1189) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 131 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Boneyard Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +19, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +19, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +22 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +3 **[Coven](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=11)** A moon hag adds [_plane shift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222), [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=268), and [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=301) to her coven's spells. --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +19 **Will** +22 **HP** 190 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) **[Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16)**
**Moonlight's Kiss** A moon hag in an area illuminated by moonlight gains a +2 status bonus to AC and initiative rolls. In the light of a full moon, she is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32), and can use the extra action only to Stride or Strike. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d12+10 slashing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 29 - **5th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=48), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=110) (at will), [Read Omens](/Spells.aspx?ID=247), [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=329) (×3), [Tongues](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) **Dreadful Prediction** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=38), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The moon hag howls a series of dreadful, apocalyptic predictions at a single creature within 30 feet, shattering its perceptions of reality. The target must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or become [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (stupefied 3 on a critical failure); regardless of the outcome, the creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. A creature that can see the moon takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saving throws against Dreadful Prediction. The stupefied condition from Dreadful Prediction persists until the curse is removed. **[Rend](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=30)** claw
Hag Harbingers Moon hags serve the cosmic forces dedicated to the end of existence. On Golarion, they serve the god [Groetus](/Deities.aspx?ID=38), the great, skull-faced moon hanging over [Pharasma's](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15). Moon hags in the Boneyard are empowered by him as they were bathed in the light of the full moon
name: Moon Hag
