<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mi-Go](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1227)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Mi-gos are both scientists and colonists, yet their extraterrestrial nature and malevolent motives color their investigations with cruelty. While their shape resembles that of an arthropod, mi-gos are in fact a highly evolved and intelligent fungus.

 In mi-go society, the pursuit of secular knowledge and religious epiphany have no distinction and inspire their pursuits in tandem. They view the [Outer Gods and Great Old Ones](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=13) of the Elder Mythos less as gods to obey and more as muses or figures of inspiration. This results in their use of a strange blend of magic and technology to create bizarre, organic items that are grown and spliced together as much as crafted in the traditional sense.

 A mi-go can survive in the void of outer space and flies through the vacuum at incredible speeds—though these journeys can take months within a single solar system and years to travel beyond. When they come to new planets to mine resources rare on their home worlds, they use clever disguises that mix technology and magic to appear as creatures of that world. While there, they select the greatest minds of that world as trophies, extracting their brains and preserving them within an eldritch cylinder that grants an awful immortality to the mind kept within.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Migo.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 6">[Mi-Go](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1227)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="NE" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Fungus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=77" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 168

**Perception** +14; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23), [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=40) (precise) 30 feet

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Mi-Go](/Languages.aspx?ID=67)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +12, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +13, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +5

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 24 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +14 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 120

</row>

**Immunities**
cold

**Weaknesses**
slashing 5

**No Breath** A mi-go doesn't breathe and is immune to effects that require breathing (such as an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126)).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d6+4 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18)

**Clever Disguises**   A mi-go can use [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) to [impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) any Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) creature, although creating such a disguise takes 1 hour. It can't impersonate a specific individual with this ability.

**Eviscerate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) The mi-go performs a swift and painful surgery on a creature it has [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33) or that is otherwise [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), attempting a [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check against the target's Fortitude DC. Regardless of the result, the target then becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target takes 6d6 slashing damage, is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round, and becomes [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (the mi-go chooses) for 24 hours. **Success** The target takes 4d6 slashing damage and is slowed 1 for 1 round by the pain. **Failure** The target takes 2d6 slashing damage. **Critical Failure** The target takes no damage.

**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)**   A mi-go deals an extra 1d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Mi-Go Language</title>

The [mi-go language](/Languages.aspx?ID=67) consists of pulsations and flashes of a wide range of colors (some of which can't be seen by humans) generated on a mi-go's head. This language can be learned by other creatures, but they cannot use it to “speak” to others without the use of illusion magic capable of generating the complex series of colors. Even then, most creatures can convey only basic notions and concepts.
</aside>
name: Mi-Go

Millindemalion are cruel fey tricksters capable of causing mayhem with their magical, mind-altering hats. Many folk tales from around the world speak of industrious fey who help struggling crafters—cobblers, milliners, tailors, and so on—during the night, creating quality wares in secret for no payment greater than a crumb of bread or a saucer of cream. These stories hold a grain of truth, as gracious fey do occasionally journey from the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) to aid a humble artisan on a whim, for bribes of food, or sometimes even as part of a concerted effort to spread beauty throughout the world. However, when an artisan becomes too reliant on this help, their friendly fey helper might become warped and twisted with resentment and neglect. Eventually, they could transform into a cruel prankster who delights in punishing mortals who dare task a fey with such mundane work. The millindemalion is the result of a kindly, hat-making fey undergoing such a transformation. Some scholars believe this erratic behavior is caused by the preponderance of quicksilver used in most millinery. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Millindemalion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1228) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 169 **Perception** +23; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +28, [Millinery Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +30, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +27 **Str** +4 **Dex** +8 **Con** +1 **Int** +7 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) felt shears_ (as [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3)) --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +27 **Will** +23 **HP** 275 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10 **[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** The millindemalion can use Hat Toss against the triggering creature instead of making a Strike, making a melee attack roll with a +27 modifier to do so.
**Unsettling Mind** Attempting to touch the frenetic mind of a millindemalion is a dangerous task. When the millindemalion succeeds at a saving throw against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect, the creature originating that effect takes 4d6 mental damage. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _felt shears_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 2d4+14 piercing plus 1d6 mental **Ranged** _felt shears_ +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=195)), **Damage** 2d4+10 piercing plus 1d6 mental **Hat Toss** ([Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The millindemalion quickly crafts a mind-altering hat in combat and tosses it onto a target with a flick of their wrist. The millindemalion chooses one of the effects below and makes a ranged attack roll with a +27 modifier and a [range increment](/Traits.aspx?ID=248) of 20 feet. On a hit, the target must succeed at a DC 33 Will saving throw or experience the listed effect for 1d4+1 rounds. If the millindemalion critically succeeds at the ranged Strike, the target takes a –4 circumstance penalty on the save. A target can only wear one millindemalion hat at a time; a new hat replaces any previous hat. The hat can't be removed before the condition ends, but when the condition ends (or on a successful save), the hat falls to pieces.
  • **Befuddling Bowler** The hat clouds the target's mind; the target becomes [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37).
  • **Bewitching Beret** The target is infatuated with their new hat and its creator, becoming [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14) by the millindemalion and the beret.
  • **Dazzling Deerstalker** The target can barely see with the hat falling down over its eyes and gains the [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) condition.
  • **Fettering Fedora** The target feels a heavy weight pressing down on them from the hat and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to their Speeds.
  • **Tiring Tricorne** The target grows sleepy and becomes [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
**[Sneak Attack](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=34)** A millindemalion deals an extra 4d6 precision damage to [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) creatures.
Similar Fey Millindemalion practice a cruel form of hat-making, but there have been sightings of similar trickster fey who can craft shoes that never stop dancing or jackets that inflict wild mood swings onto their victims.
name: Millindemalion

Mobogos are massive, swamp-dwelling monstrosities that combine the worst aspects of giant toads and evil dragons. Lazy, cruel, and greedy, these vile creatures make their lairs in the most ancient and primordial swamps. The [boggards](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=16) who call such places home worship mobogos as living demigods, regularly bringing sacrifices of food and valuables lest they become the next victims of the mobogos' boundless appetites. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Mobogo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1229) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 170 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Boggard](/Languages.aspx?ID=27); [_speak with animals_](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +23, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** -2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +17 **Will** +19 **HP** 160 , regeneration 30 (deactivated by acid, cold, or fire) --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 20 feet, swim 30 feet; swamp stride **Melee** jaws +23 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+13 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** tongue +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+13 bludgeoning plus tongue grab **Primal Innate Spells** DC 27 - **4th** [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=53) (at will), [Entangle](/Spells.aspx?ID=103), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), [Sound Burst](/Spells.aspx?ID=292) (at will) - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=51) - **Constant (2nd)** [Pass Without Trace](/Spells.aspx?ID=215), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=293) **Rituals** DC 27 - **4th** [Plant Growth](/Rituals.aspx?ID=18) **Song of the Swamp** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The mobogo unleashes a booming croak. All boggards and mobogos within 50 feet gain a +2 status bonus to damage rolls and saves against fear for 1 round. Other creatures in the area of effect must attempt a DC 27 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 2 for 1d4 rounds. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=35)** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15)) Large, 2d12+6 bludgeoning, Rupture 19 **Swamp Stride** A mobogo ignores difficult terrain caused by swamp terrain features. **Tongue Grab** A creature hit by the mobogo's tongue becomes [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the mobogo. The creature isn't [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24), but it can't move beyond the reach of the mobogo's tongue. A creature can sever the tongue with a Strike against AC 27 that deals at least 10 slashing damage. This deals no damage to the mobogo but prevents it from using its tongue Strike until it regrows its tongue, which takes 1 round. The mobogo can move without ending the Grab as long as the creature remains within the tongue's reach. **Tongue Reposition** The mobogo attempts to move a creature grabbed by its tongue. The mobogo rolls an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the mobogo moves the creature into any space within the tongue's reach. If it wishes, the mobogo can transfer the grabbed creature to its jaws.
Children of Gogunta The [boggards](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=16) of Golarion believe mobogos to have hatched from the first clutch of eggs laid by their demon goddess [Gogunta](/Deities.aspx?ID=25), following her awakening at the dawn of creation. The boggards, hatched millennia later from the second clutch, have been charged with serving and aiding their elder siblings in keeping her sacred swamplands untainted by the presence of outsiders.
name: Mobogo

Mokele-mbembes are large, reptilian predators found deep within Golarion's jungles. Mwangi view mokele-mbembes as embodiments of nature's strength and majesty, and they consider the sighting of this rare being a sign of favor from the gods and an omen of powerful natural forces. Mokele-mbembes are solitary and excel at going undetected. Most information about them—including the fact that they're voracious predators—comes from following the signs of their passage. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Mokele-Mbembe](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1230) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 171, [Pathfinder #146: Cult of Cinders](/Sources.aspx?ID=7) pg. 90 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +17 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +15 **Will** +17 **HP** 172 --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +22 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d12+10 piercing **Melee** tail +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+10 bludgeoning **Melee** foot +22 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d8+10 bludgeoning **[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** Large or smaller, foot, DC 28 **Whip Tail** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The mokele-mbembe cracks its tail, creating a sonic boom in a 5-foot burst centered on a corner within reach of its tail Strike. Each creature in the burst's area must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save. Mokele-mbembes are immune.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).
**Failure** The creature is stunned 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stunned 3.
Locations Mokele-mbembes live in tropical jungles close to lakes, rivers, and other bodies of water. They take great care to hide themselves from civilization and other large predators, thus it can take many weeks or months of searching to locate even just their footprints. Travelers rarely encounter mokele-mbembes by accident, but those who do so (and live to tell about it) are considered very lucky indeed.
name: Mokele-Mbembe

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Moose](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1233)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Moose are a large, solitary type of deer. They're majestic, territorial, and extremely grumpy—traits that make them dangerous to unwary travelers and would-be predators alike. Males are instantly recognizable, standing 7 feet tall at the shoulder, with large heads and thick necks to support their impressive palmate antlers. They shed their antlers come winter and grow a new set each year at a rapid rate.

 For most of the year, moose roam tundra, forest, and wilderness territories by themselves. Their hooves are wide, allowing them to more easily traverse the frequent snowfall of their native terrain, and they typically journey alone. Moose come together only during mating season, a time in which they become oddly social with one another, loud, and even more aggressive to other creatures than usual.

 A moose's diet consists of leaves, woody plant matter like twigs or bark, and buds of trees and shrubs. Moose are so large they prefer to eat the leaves of trees taller than humans instead of lowering their giant heads all the way to the ground. This can make them easy to track, though hunters will find actually killing a moose is a far greater challenge than finding it. Despite their size, moose have many natural predators, especially pack animals like wolves and large carnivores like tigers. Large humanoids like [ogres](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=78) and [trolls](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=96) dwelling in the wilderness sometimes make sport of chasing down and wrestling moose.

 Despite their large size, moose are expert swimmers in both fresh and salt water, and they readily feed on aquatic vegetation. Moose rarely swim into open seas, however, and usually stay close to land, though ocean predators have been known to hunt swimming moose. To an observer watching from the safe distance of a dry shore, the large branching antlers of a swimming moose can sometimes be mistaken for a tree bobbing along in the water.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Moose.png" />
</column>

</row>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Languages**

<row gap="medium">

**Dex** +3

</column>

---

**AC** 18

</row>

**Cold Adaptation** The moose reduces the effects it suffers from [cold environments](/Rules.aspx?ID=642) by one step.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
antler +12,
**Damage** 1d10+7 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
hoof +12,
**Damage** 1d8+7 bludgeoning

**Kick Back** <actions string="Single Action" />  The moose bucks and kicks back with both hind hooves, making a Strike with a –2 circumstance penalty to the attack roll. If it hits, it deals an extra 1d8 bludgeoning damage. This counts as two attacks when calculating the moose's multiple attack penalty.

**Thundering Charge** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The moose Strides twice and then makes an antler Strike. A Medium or smaller creature damaged by this attack must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save or be [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36).

**[Trample](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=39)** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Medium or smaller, hoof, DC 20

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_3_Locations.png">Moose Tracks</title>

Moose thrive in cooler climates and have many adaptations to survive in the cold, including thick skin and dense, heat-retaining fur. They often make their own trails in the snow to find the best food. Adventurers sometimes stumble upon these trails and assume they lead to shelter, only to find themselves happening upon a fiercely territorial moose.
</aside>
name: Moose

Angheuvores, or mortic [elves](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2), have an insatiable hunger for raw [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) flesh— though they're reluctant to feed on other elves. Most stalk the streets of human nations, such as Druma, Razmiran, and the River Kingdoms. Impatient and impulsive, they're opportunistic hunters, preying on the wounded and isolated. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Angheuvore Flesh-Gnawer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1234) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 174 **Perception** +7; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), carrion scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=4), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +7, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +5, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +5, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +8, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +5 **Str** +3 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +1 **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), [Elven Curve Blade](/Weapons.aspx?ID=50) --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +10 **Will** +5 **HP** 30 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Consecration Vulnerability** An angheuvore in a place of worship dedicated to a non-evil deity or on sacred ground, such as an area blessed by [_sanctified ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35). --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** jaws +10 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing plus paralysis **Melee** elven curve blade +10 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=180)), **Damage** 1d8+5 slashing **Ranged** composite shortbow +10 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+3 piercing **Death Gasp** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The angheuvore draws in a deep breath and holds it, temporarily suspending their biological processes and becoming undead. The angheuvore gains the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) trait and becomes immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Any such effects the angheuvore is currently suffering from are suspended, but take effect again once they take a breath.
Death Gasp lasts as long as the angheuvore [holds their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (up to 8 rounds). **Invigorating Feast** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Requirements** The angheuvore is adjacent to a [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=33), or [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) creature, or a deceased creature that died in the last hour; **Effect** The angheuvore feasts upon the creature, dealing their jaws Strike damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save); a corpse automatically critically fails with no save. If the target takes damage, the angheuvore gains 5 temporary Hit Points and a +10-foot circumstance bonus to their Speeds for 1 minute. **Paralysis** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Any living creature hit by an angheuvore's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or become [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). It can attempt a new save to end the condition at the end of each of its turns, and the DC cumulatively decreases by 1 on each save.
Scholarly Pursuits Angheuvores retain the elven love of education, although the topics that interest them are decidedly macabre. Many study anatomy, haruspicy, medicine, pain, putrefaction, or trauma, preferring hands-on experiments and research whenever possible.
name: Angheuvore Flesh-Gnawer

markdown:
[Humans](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6) that become mortics are known as gurgists. They retain their personalities and intelligence, but their bodies rot with every beat of their heart. Gurgists ceaselessly crave raw meat; consuming large quantities can slow, or even reverse, their body's putrefaction for a short time. A gurgist that refuses to consume raw meat rots to death. Unlike other mortics, gurgists are capable of sating their hunger without resorting to depravity, making them more likely to be nonevil compared to other mortics. Most gurgists hunt and raise livestock for food—including animals many consider too dangerous to tame. To battle their constant hunger, many take up a single craft, hobby, or performing art to obsess over until the drive to create overcomes the urge to consume. Many study magic or medicine, researching methods to reverse their condition or suspend their putrescence. Others cling to religion, uttering prayers despite the pain and begging the gods to cleanse them of evil. Nearly all gurgists are disgusted by their clumsy, rotting bodies. They shroud themselves in fine clothes and cover their faces with elaborate masks. Most consider their mask their true face. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Gurgist Mauler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1235) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 175 **Perception** +14; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) +15, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +14, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) +12, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Nourishing Feast** The gurgist spends 1 hour consuming 1 Bulk of raw meat to reverse their body's putrescence. For 1 hour, the gurgist appears to be a pale, bloated human. They have an automatic result of 32 on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks and DCs to pass as a non-mortic [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6). While under the effects of Nourishing Feast, Rotting Flesh ceases to function. **Items** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows), [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=7), _+1 [maul](/Weapons.aspx?ID=33)_ --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +12 **Will** +16 **HP** 120 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Weaknesses** slashing 5 **Consecration Vulnerability** A gurgist in a place of worship dedicated to a non-evil deity or on sacred ground, such as an area blessed by [_sanctified ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
**Rotting Flesh** Any creature that hits the gurgist with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack, tries to [Grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184) them, or otherwise touches the gurgist is covered with rotten flesh and putrid fluids. The creature must succeed at a DC 22 Fortitude save or become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) (sickened 2 on a critical failure). --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _maul_ +18 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193)), **Damage** 1d12+8 bludgeoning plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=20) **Melee** fist +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** composite shortbow +14 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+5 piercing **Calculated Blow** The gurgist makes a melee Strike, aiming for their target's weak points and taking their time to line up a precise blow. If this Strike hits, the gurgist deals an additional 1d8 precision damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 23 Fortitude save or become [clumsy 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 minute. **Death Gasp** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The gurgist draws in a deep breath and holds it, temporarily suspending their biological processes and becoming undead. The gurgist gains the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) trait and becomes immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Any such effects the gurgist is currently suffering from are suspended, but take effect again once they take a breath.
Death Gasp lasts as long as the gurgist [holds their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (up to 9 rounds). **Tackle** The gurgist Strides twice and then attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) a creature. On a success, the target is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
name: Gurgist Mauler

markdown:
Lifeleechers are mortic [orcs](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=19) with gray flesh, sharp claws, and protruding tusks. Resilient and strong, they devour the vital essence of their enemies, which heals their wounds and shackles those souls to the lifeleecher. Many orcs believe lifeleechers are possessed by the spirits of fallen orc warriors too stubborn to die. Feared and revered, lifeleechers often rise to positions of power in their holds and battle on the front lines against the undead armies of the Whispering Tyrant. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Lifeleecher Brawler](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1236) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 176 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10), _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=75) (20 arrows)_ --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 165 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Consecration Vulnerability** A lifeleecher in a place of worship dedicated to a non-evil deity or on sacred ground, such as an area blessed by [_sanctified ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
**Mortic Ferocity** As [Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=16), but the lifeleecher is also surrounded by visibly flickering fragments of the souls they've consumed, becoming [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) until the end of their next turn.
**Soul Feast** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) **Trigger** A creature adjacent to the lifeleecher dies; **Effect** The lifeleecher consumes a portion of the creature's soul, regaining 2d8 Hit Points. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** tusk +20, **Damage** 2d6+6 piercing plus leech essence **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d8+6 slashing plus 1d6 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) **Ranged** _composite shortbow_ +19 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [Propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=191), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+3 piercing **Death Gasp** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The lifeleecher draws in a deep breath and holds it, temporarily suspending their biological processes and becoming undead. The lifeleecher gains the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) trait and becomes immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Any such effects the lifeleecher is currently suffering from are suspended, but take effect again once they take a breath.
Death Gasp lasts as long as the lifeleecher [holds their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (up to 11 rounds). **Ghost Hunter** The lifeleecher's tusks have the effects of the _[ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297)_ property rune on attacks against [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160). **Leech Essence** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the lifeleecher damages a living or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creature with their jaws Strike, they drain the target's life essence. The lifeleecher gains 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute, and the target must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) if living, or [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) if undead. If the target was already drained or stunned, the value of this condition instead increases by 1, to a maximum of 3.
Defying Death Lifeleechers are commonly formed when an orc tainted by the pollutants of the Gravelands falls in battle. As their ferocity overcomes them, they transform into mortics and continue the fight, drawing sustenance from the enemies that first laid them low.
name: Lifeleecher Brawler

markdown:
When the primal spark of the [First World](/Planes.aspx?ID=10) nestled in a [gnome's](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=3) heart is engulfed in _Radiant Fire_, it fights back, pushing against death with riotous life and creating an etioling. Within their small frames, churning positive and negative energy fiercely battle for dominance, occasionally causing convulsions and babbling speech. This intense inner conflict warps an etioling's primal magic, transmogrifying it into an occult hybrid singular to these unfortunate mortics. Nearly devoid of color, etiolings have vibrant eyes and pulsing green veins. Their bodies force the corrupting negative energy out through their pores, where it distills on their skin, covering them in a thin layer of ectoplasm and concentrated death. Around them, the world withers. Incapable of focusing for long, etiolings are impulsive and moody, rapidly alternating between kindness, malice, and trickery. They forget themselves, their friends, and the things they once loved. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Etioling Blightmage](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1237) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 177 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +22, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +19 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +21 **Will** +17 **HP** 150 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42)) **Consecration Vulnerability** An etioling in a place of worship dedicated to a non-evil deity or on sacred ground, such as an area blessed by [_sanctified ground_](/Spells.aspx?ID=265), is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35).
**Ectoplasmic Secretions** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) Any creature that hits the etioling with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack, tries to [Grapple](/Traits.aspx?ID=184) them, or otherwise touches them becomes partially coated in ectoplasm. The creature takes 1d6 negative damage and must succeed at a DC 27 Reflex save or become [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) until the ectoplasm is removed. The ectoplasm can be removed with a total of 3 Interact actions by the creature or creatures adjacent to the creature. These actions don't need to be consecutive or made by the same creature.
**Ectoplasmic Shield** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) **Trigger** The etioling is the target of a physical ranged attack; **Effect** The etioling interposes a wave of ectoplasm between themself and the source of the ranged attack, giving them a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the attack misses, the ectoplasm deflected it. The ectoplasm can't deflect unusually large or heavy ranged projectiles (such as boulders or ballista bolts).
**Withering Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) 20 feet. The etioling drains nutrients from nearby plant and animal life. Each round a creature begins its turn in this aura, it takes 1d6 negative damage and must succeed at a DC 27 Fortitude save or become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). All non-magical plant life in this aura instantly withers, removing any cover and concealment provided by trees and undergrowth. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** corrupting touch +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 2d12+6 negative **Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Acid Splash](/Spells.aspx?ID=3), [Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=97), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=229) - **1st** [Bane](/Spells.aspx?ID=18), [Grease](/Spells.aspx?ID=140), [Ray of Enfeeblement](/Spells.aspx?ID=244), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=356) (4 slots) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=28), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Glitterdust](/Spells.aspx?ID=136), [Obscuring Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=210) (4 slots) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=94), [False Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=108), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=147), [Wall of Thorns](/Spells.aspx?ID=366) (4 slots) - **4th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [Freedom of Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=128), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=141), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (4 slots) - **5th** [Cloudkill](/Spells.aspx?ID=42), [Crushing Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=57), [Lightning Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=172), [Synesthesia](/Spells.aspx?ID=328) (4 slots) **Death Gasp** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) The etioling draws in a deep breath and holds it, temporarily suspending their biological processes and becoming undead. The etioling gains the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) trait and becomes immune to bleed, [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), and [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145). Any such effects the etioling is currently suffering from are suspended, but take effect again once they take a breath.
Additionally, the etioling gains the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) trait, a fly Speed of 25 feet, resistance 10 to all damage (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double this resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) while they hold their breath. They can't cast spells during this time.
Death Gasp lasts as long as the etioling [holds their breath](/Rules.aspx?ID=468) (up to 8 rounds).
name: Etioling Blightmage

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Platecarpus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1238)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The mid-sized platecarpuses are the most common type of mosasaur. They're typically 15 feet long, but larger specimens can grow up to 20 feet. The larger varieties sometimes eat Medium humanoids, but most stick to smaller fare.

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Mothman](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1240)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Little is known of the strange creatures known as mothmen, save that when they make themselves known, calamity is sure to follow. The mothman sees themself as an agent of fate—provided such fate is devastating and destructive. Often, those who encounter a mothman never recall the meeting, yet live on to unknowingly pursue and enable the creature's obscure plans.

 The name “mothmen” is, of course, not these creatures' own name for themselves, but an appellation granted by the fearful who glimpse them and tell harrowing tales of their sightings. They each keep their own names, or a collection for use in different situations, yet have no need for a collective word for their kind.

<column gap="tiny">

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Mothman](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1240)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="CN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Aberration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=1" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 179

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)

**Languages**
[Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); (can't speak any language); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +13, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +4

**Int** +2

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +5
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 26 

**Fort** +13 

**Ref** +18 

**Will** +15 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 90

</row>

**Portentous Gaze** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) 30 feet. When a creature ends its turn in the aura, it must attempt a DC 25 Will save. A creature that fails is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (or frightened 2 on a critical failure) The mothman can deactivate or activate this aura with a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 50 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +16 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d8+4 slashing

**Occult Innate Spells** DC 25, attack +17
- **Cantrips (4th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=61), [Ghost Sound](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=246)
- **2nd**
[Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=160), [Misdirection](/Spells.aspx?ID=198)
- **3rd**
[Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=158), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=194)
- **4th**
[Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=164), [Modify Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=200), [Nightmare](/Spells.aspx?ID=208), [Phantasmal Killer](/Spells.aspx?ID=219), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=315)

**Agent of Fate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The mothman casts any spell of 5th level or lower that normally takes 2 or fewer actions to cast, whether they have those spells in their innate spells or not. They can use this ability only if doing so steers the flow of fate in its proper course toward a disaster or otherwise significantly memorable event. What the proper flow of fate entails is determined by the GM, but some examples include casting [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=161) to coax someone to a portentous location, casting [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272) to deliver an important message to someone the exact moment they need to hear it, or casting [_rusting grasp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=711) to weaken a structure and cause some necessary calamity.

**Focus Gaze** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) The mothman fixes their glare at a creature they can see within 30 feet. The target must immediately attempt a Will save against the mothman's portentous gaze. If the creature is already [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) when it attempts this save, on a failure it becomes [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 24 hours in addition to the frightened effect. After attempting its save, the creature is then temporarily immune for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">The Red Bishop</title>

Primary among mothmen on Golarion is the Red Bishop, a priest of [Pazuzu](/Deities.aspx?ID=79) whose mysterious plans for the small town of Sandpoint in Varisia hinge on the collection and manipulation of adventuring groups. The Red Bishop seeks to cultivate a group that will someday, albeit unknowingly, serve as a final catalyst to unleash a devastating, hidden plot upon the region.
</aside>

Although the subterranean Darklands are known for their cruel and domineering civilizations—led by fiend-worshipping [drow](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=37), [urdefhans](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=158), and others—that dwell within those sinister caverns, not every such subterranean society is ruled that way. markdown:
The ambulatory fungus creatures called myceloids are notorious for spreading deadly purple pox, controlling creatures' minds, and devouring [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) flesh. For a myceloid colony, any battle with humanoids is cause for excitement, as this new fodder presents so many delicious possibilities. Myceloids consider humanoids to have an ideal life cycle of four simple steps. During childhood, they wander naive and afraid, unaware of myceloid colonies. In adulthood, humanoids discover their true purpose as they taste purple pox and become enslaved by the myceloids' spores. Next, they die, giving rise to a new myceloid. They're then sent on to the afterlife upon becoming a myceloid meal. Eating humanoid flesh is not a necessity—a myceloid can survive on any decaying matter—but it's certainly a pleasure. Myceloids rarely make alliances, but when they do, they invite their new allies to share a meal to seal the pact. Few outsiders appreciate this hospitality. Most myceloids have deep purple caps studded with off-white lumps. Their necks and bodies bear resemblance to the stipes of tough, leathery fungi. Smaller mushrooms often grow on a myceloid's body, which the creatures view as either adornments or particularly convenient snacks. They stand roughly as tall as a [dwarf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=1), with comparably stout builds. **[Recall Knowledge - Fungus](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Myceloid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1242) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 181 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Undercommon](/Languages.aspx?ID=11); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet (myceloids and those afflicted by purple pox only) **Skills** [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +10 **Str** +4 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -1 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +9 **Will** +10 **HP** 70 **Weaknesses** slashing 5 **Spore Pop** If a myceloid is reduced to 0 HP by a critical hit, it pops, forcing it to immediately Emit Spores, even if it has already used the ability that day. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +14, **Damage** 2d6+4 bludgeoning plus purple pox **Emit Spores** **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The myceloid expels spores in a 10-foot burst centered on a corner of its own space. This cloud lasts until the start of the myceloid's next turn. Each creature that is in the cloud or enters it is exposed to purple pox. **Purple Pox** ([Disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46)) Myceloids are immune; **Saving Throw** DC 20 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) (1 day); **Stage 2** 6d6 poison damage, stupefied 3, and the creature is compelled to seek out the nearest myceloid colony—this compulsion is a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60) effect (1 day); **Stage 3** The creature dies. Over 24 hours, its corpse becomes bloated and bursts, releasing a new, fully grown myceloid. **Spore Domination** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) The myceloid targets one creature affected by purple pox within 60 feet. That creature must attempt a DC 22 Will save. It is then temporarily immune to spore domination for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** Until the end of its next turn, the target is [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21) to myceloids and can't take [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against them.
**Failure** As success, but for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but until the purple pox is cured.
Table manners Myceloids eat communal meals consisting of a series of corpses, beginning with creatures they killed in combat, followed by any that died in service while enslaved with spore domination. As they dine, they offer tasting notes, claiming they can taste intangibles like innocence or despair. They save any creature that died from purple pox as dessert. The new myceloid birthed from the corpse gets the first slice!

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Nagaji are brawny bipeds with humanoid figures and serpentine heads. Their bodies are covered in tightly layered scales that range in color from green to brown, and almost all nagaji have a colorful ridge along their head and back. Ophidian eyes lend nagaji an imperious visage, with irises that span every color of the rainbow

markdown:
Namorrodor stalk the night, craving the flesh of the living. Though they dwell on the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11), the shadowy substances of that plane offer them only the barest sustenance, and they forever seek to cross over onto the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1)— yet they can make this journey only on the eve of a death, and only in a place where their plane overlaps the Material. When they manage to make the leap between planes, a shooting star marks their passage. Attracted by the smell of cooking meat but particularly vulnerable to flame, a namorrodor's most common meal is a traveler who has strayed from their campfire. Unattended babies are a treat so delectable, however, that a namorrodor will risk an encounter with dreaded fire if it spies an opportunity to snatch one. A namorrodor's skin is torn and loose over jutting bones, through which the wind whistles with a distinctive, eerie tone. Able to change between a quadrupedal and bipedal stance, the namorrodor is an admirable hunter. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 20 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15 [Namorrodor](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1244) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 183 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=21) 30 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +14, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=16) +11 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Interplanar Lifesense** While the namorrodor is on the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11), its lifesense extends to the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) as a vague sense with a 100-foot range. --- **AC** 22 **Fort** +12 **Ref** +14 **Will** +11 **HP** 85 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Weaknesses** positive 5, fire 5 **Whistling Bones** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 30 feet. Wind whistles through the namorrodor's jutting bones with an eerie tone. A creature entering or beginning its turn in the area must attempt a DC 19 Will save, becoming [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) on a failure (frightened 2 on a critical failure). A creature can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 as long as it's in the aura.
**Material Leap** ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) **Requirements** The namorrodor is on the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) <%END>; **Trigger** A creature the namorrodor can sense with its lifesense on the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1) dies; **Effect** The namorrodor leaps between planes, appearing on the Material Plane within 100 feet of the triggering creature. It remains on the Material Plane for 24 hours, after which it is recalled to its original location on the Shadow Plane.
 --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** jaws +15, **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing **Melee** claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+6 piercing plus Grab **Ranged** spit +14 ([range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d8+6 bludgeoning **Bounding Sprint** **Requirements** The namorrodor has nothing in its hands; **Effect** The namorrodor bounds forward, using four limbs for an extra burst of speed. It Strides twice and makes a single melee Strike at the end of its movement.
Night Stalkers Since living on the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11) gives them easy access to broad areas of the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1), tales of namorrodors are told all over the world, from the Storval Plateau's river gorges to the Bandu Hills in northwestern Vidrian, Casmaron's Windswept Wastes, and the river dividing Hongal from the Shaguang desert in Tian Xia. Their names vary, but reports of shooting stars heralding their arrival and their eerie whistling auras are remarkably consistent.
name: Namorrodor

markdown:
Narwhals are mid-sized whales known for the long tusk that grows from their nose. The tusk, forming from a protruding canine tooth, appears primarily on males and can grow up to 10 feet in length over the course of an adult narwhal's life. In addition to helping the narwhal attract mates (much like a peacock's feathers), the tusk can serve as a weapon. Like dolphins, narwhals communicate via clicks and whistles, and they use sound to navigate and hunt. They congregate in groups of five to 10, except in the summer when they gather in the hundreds. Adult narwhals are typically 13–18 feet long (not counting the tusk) and weigh 1,500–3,500 pounds. The similarities between a narwhal's tusk and a unicorn's horn have led to the narwhals' nickname of “sea unicorns,” but the narwhal's tusk is not made of the same highly sought material. Legends surrounding narwhals have nevertheless attributed magical characteristics to these remarkable tusks, such as neutralizing poison. Collectors and artisans also value the tusks as an elegant crafting material useful for carvings and other works of art. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 18 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 [Narwhal](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1245) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 184 **Perception** +10; aquatic echolocation 120 feet, [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +10 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** -4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Aquatic Echolocation** A narwhal can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range, but only underwater.
**Deep Breath** A narwhal can hold its breath for 30 minutes. --- **AC** 18 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +10 **Will** +8 **HP** 50 **Resistances** cold 5 --- **Speed** swim 60 feet **Melee** tusk +12 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 1d10+5 piercing **Melee** jaws +12, **Damage** 1d8+5 piercing **Impaling Charge** The narwhal Swims twice and makes a tusk Strike. If it moved at least 20 feet from its starting position, the narwhal deals an extra 1d10 damage on this Strike. A target damaged by Impaling Charge is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) until the start of the narwhal's next turn unless it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) before then (DC 20). The narwhal can't [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) to extend the duration of this grab.
Narwhals as Mounts Some aquatic or amphibious beings capture narwhals for use as mounts, making use of their speed, endurance, and tusk attack. As narwhals are simple animals, they serve riders as varied as [locathahs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=281), [sea devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=88), [sea hags](/Monsters.aspx?ID=254), and [tritons](/Monsters.aspx?ID=830).
name: Narwhal

This undead guardian stands amid a whirling swarm of shackled spirits that rage against their fate. Bound by a profane necromantic ritual, a nemhaith and its servitors maintain watch over an object or place so important they embraced undeath to guard it. This ritual binds connected beings—friends, family, fellow worshippers, or even sacrificed innocents—to form a single nemhaith. Most nemhaiths were, in life, priests or protectors who sought immortality to protect the temple they served or guard an important landmark. Others follow an endless and even impossible quest beyond the bounds of life and death. A nemhaith is not always evil at first, but the inherent evil and violence of a nemhaith's existence inevitably darkens even the brightest light. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 36 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 36 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 34 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 [Nemhaith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1246) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 185 **Perception** +31; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=37) 100 feet **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +27, [Dwelling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +29, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +31, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +29 **Str** -5 **Dex** +6 **Con** +0 **Int** +4 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 **Site Bound** The nemhaith is bound to a place or large stationary object and can't venture more than 500 feet from this focus. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +27 **Will** +29 **HP** 255 ([negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), rejuvenation) **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-[magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)) **Bound Spirits** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) 10 feet, 4d6 negative, DC 36 basic Fortitude
**Rejuvenation** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) Destroying the ritual object or place used in the nemhaith's creation destroys the nemhaith, freeing its bound spirits to move on to the afterlife. --- **Speed** fly 40 feet **Melee** spirit tendril +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103)), **Damage** 3d10+14 negative plus drain life **Ranged** spirit dart +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d8+14 negative plus drain life **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36, attack +28 - **8th** [Finger of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=116) (×3), [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=146) (×3), [Wall of Force](/Spells.aspx?ID=363) **Drain Life** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) When the nemhaith damages a living creature with its spirit tendril Strike, the creature must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or become [drained 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10). Further damage dealt by the nemhaith increases the condition value by 1 on a failed save, to a maximum of drained 4. **Rage of Spirits** The nemhaith's bound spirits rage and smash everything in a 30-foot emanation. Creatures in this area take 4d6 sonic damage and 4d6 negative damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save). **Throw Spirits** The nemhaith directs its bound spirits as a group to move up to 1 mile away (they have a fly Speed of 60 feet). The nemhaith can see and hear through the thrown spirits, but it loses its aura and can't use its spirit dart ranged Strike or Rage of Spirits. The nemhaith regains these abilities when the spirits return to the nemhaith's space.
Captive Spirits The ritual to form a nemhaith doesn't require consent from its spirits, which might come as a shocking betrayal. A nemhaith can grow more powerful as living creatures drained of life by its touch join its coterie of spirits.
name: Nemhaith

Nightgaunts dwell in dreams, lurking in wait to spirit away the unwary dreamer. Once connected to such a being, the nightgaunt feeds upon the mortal's emotions before abandoning them—often in places they might never wake from. A nightgaunt appears as a bony humanoid with inky black skin, batlike wings, a long sinuous tail, and demonic horns atop a skull that lacks any face or features. Most nightgaunts have little interest in anything other than sating their hunger for emotions. They find the art of inducing nightmares to be a surefire way to feast, but they're even more adept at tormenting their victims to awful extremes through tickling while carrying them at precarious heights. Nightgaunts often gather in vast colonies in the [Dreamlands](/Planes.aspx?ID=22), where they entertain each other by sharing emotion-memories of their meals through strange caresses. These colonies pose great danger to any adventurer foolish enough to approach. When conjured forth into other worlds, they serve only grudgingly, often working as hard to find a way to escape servitude and feed on their conjurer's emotions as they do on the task they've been compelled to perform. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 21 **[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Nightgaunt](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1247) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 186 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), thoughtsense (precise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +11 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** -2 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +0 **Thoughtsense** ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=120)) A nightgaunt senses all non-[mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=108) creatures at the listed range. --- **AC** 21 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=1) **Fort** +10 **Ref** +13 **Will** +10 **HP** 60 **Resistances** cold 5 **Faceless** The nightgaunt has no face, but it can still see in all directions as if its entire body were an eye. It has no need to breathe, and it is immune to all [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=96) toxins and other [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=246) effects.
**[Attack of Opportunity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=3)** Tail only. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** claw +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 2d6+7 plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) **Melee** tail +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** tickle **Clutches** A nightgaunt can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) at full Speed while it has a Medium or smaller creature grabbed or restrained in its claws, carrying that creature along with it. **Tickle** The nightgaunt can use its tail to tickle a foe with horrible efficiency. A creature hit by its tail Strike must attempt a DC 21 Fortitude save; if the creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the nightgaunt, it uses the outcome one degree of success worse than the result it rolled.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature is overcome with laughter and can't perform reactions for 1 round.
**Failure** As success, and the creature is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Critical Failure** As success, and the creature is sickened 2 and can't speak for 1 round.
Emotion eaters Nightgaunts feed on emotions via touch, preferring unique cocktails formed of conflicting emotions, especially despair, horror, or nervous laughter. Such feeding has little lasting impact on their food source, but a nightgaunt can only feed on a given creature once. As a result, they tend to satiate themselves before seeking out another food source.
name: Nightgaunt

While smooth roads remain a traveler's blessing, walking them after sunset risks nightmarcher attack. The ringing of conch shells and beating of drums herald the coming of these spirits as they walk the royal highways, unfettered by walls or barriers. Nightmarchers react violently to those in their path. If a bystander is fortunate, an ancestor's spirit might call out to spare them. Those who refuse to show proper deference might be reduced to little more than ash on the wind come sunrise. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11)): DC 32 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Nightmarchers](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1248) **Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 187 **Perception** +27; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12), kinsense **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +25, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) +27, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) +27 **Str** -5 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 **Kinsense** ([detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=43), [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48)) Nightmarchers can detect creatures who are their kin, whether by blood or bond, as an imprecise sense. If they focus their senses on a creature by [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84), they learn whether they are related to that creature, and how. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +19 **Will** +25 **HP** 240 **Immunities** [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126), precision, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=145) **Resistances** all damage 5 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75), [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), or [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=128); double resistance vs. non-magical) **Weaknesses** area damage 20, [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=150) damage 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=17)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) 90 feet, DC 31
**[Troop Defenses](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=44)** --- **Speed** 25 feet; [_air walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=6), troop movement **Divine Innate Spells** DC 31 - **Constant (4th)** [Air Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=6) **Blazing Admonition** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=48), [Evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163)) Heat scorches those who lay eyes on the nightmarchers. All creatures in a 60-foot cone take 15d6 fire damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). [Prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) creatures and the nightmarchers' kin are unaffected as long as they have not taken a [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against the nightmarchers. The nightmarchers can't use Blazing Admonition for 1d4 rounds. **[Form Up](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=43)** **Missile Volley** The nightmarchers fling a hail of spears and stones, dealing 5d6+9 bludgeoning or piercing damage to creatures in a 10-foot burst within 20 feet (DC 31 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). When the nightmarchers are reduced to 8 or fewer squares, this decreases to a 5-foot burst. **Striking Koa** **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The troop attacks with spears, clubs, and leiomano against enemies within 5 feet (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save) for their choice of bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage depending on the number of actions.
2d6+2 slashing damage
4d6+12 slashing damage
6d6+12 slashing damage **Troop Movement** Whenever the nightmarchers Stride, they first Form Up as a free action to condense into a 20-foot-by-20-foot area (minus any missing squares), then move. This works just like a Gargantuan creature moving; for instance, if any of the nightmarchers' squares enter difficult terrain, the extra movement cost applies to the whole group.
Avoidance and Deference True to their name, nightmarchers tend to walk royal highways only after nightfall, making daytime travel safer. While they can traverse virtually any barrier, they can't cross ti leaves or similar sacred plants. If one finds themself in nightmarchers' presence, the last recourse is to show proper deference by lying flat on the ground with eyes shut—if one hears a ghostly voice call out to claim them, it means that an ancestor is present among the nightmarchers and has directed the procession to spare the bystander from harm.
name: Nightmarchers

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Lampad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1249)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Lampads protect dark, hidden places underground. Not only do they defend subterranean caverns from threats, but they also safeguard well-meaning innocents from the dangers that lurk below the surface. Lampads are so-named for the wisps of magic light they often carry, guiding the lost to safety while luring threats to their doom. Lampads' mercurial nature makes their reactions difficult to predict, though they rarely demonstrate outright malice without sufficient provocation.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Nymph_Lampad.png" />
</column>

</row>

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5

**Cavern Dependent** A lampad is mystically bonded to a single cavern or other self-contained underground area and must remain within 300 feet of it. If they move beyond that range, they become [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) and are unable to recover. They must attempt a DC 19 Fortitude save every hour or increase the sickened value by 1 (to a maximum of sickened 4). After 24 hours, they become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), with this value increasing by 1 every additional 24 hours. A lampad can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond to a new cavern.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
earthen fist +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d10+2 bludgeoning

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
light wisp +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 1d8+2 mental plus 1d6 fire and 1d6 positive

- **2nd**

- **4th**
[Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=276)

**Weep** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [Enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The lampad begins a heart-wrenching fit of weeping, inspiring sympathetic sobbing in nearby creatures. Each non-lampad creature within 30 feet who hears the lampad's weeping must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or be unable to use reactions for 1 round and [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) on its next turn as it sobs uncontrollably

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Strong Emotions</title>

While lampads are ever vigilant in their assigned tasks, they're known to become lonely and forlorn, as the majority of underground denizens make poor company. True companionship and conversation are among the few things that can keep a moody lampad from sporadically weeping, though like most creatures they find such tears cathartic, feeling better for a time after a good cry.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-77" />
name: Lampad

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Hesperid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1250)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Hesperides are nymphs of sunset, guardians of the colorful golden hues of the setting sun. They live on remote islands, isolated coastal cliffsides, and hidden valleys, all places where the sunset's golden glow can have the strongest effect. Hesperides manipulate sunlight with dancelike motions, allowing them to create graceful ribbons of light at close range and searing rays at a distance. Due to their connection to the beauty of the daily cycle of the setting sun, hesperides feel a satisfaction from methodical routine that can feel alien to wilder, more chaotic fey. 

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Nymph_Hesperid.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Hesperid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1250)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="LN" url="/Rules.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Fey" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=69" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Nymph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=230" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Bestiary 3](/Sources.aspx?ID=66) pg. 188

**Perception** +19; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) +11, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) +21, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) +19, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) +19, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) +17

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +0

**Dex** +6

**Con** +4

**Int** +4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +6
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 28 

**Fort** +15 

**Ref** +21 

**Will** +19 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 175

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 10

**Sunset Dependent** A hesperid is mystically bonded to a single remote location with a good view of the sunset—usually an island, coastal cliff, or valley. If they aren't at that location and able to see the sky at sunset on any given day, they become [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), increasing the value by 1 for each missed sunset and reducing by 1 only when they see the sunset. A hesperid can perform a 24-hour ritual to bond to a new location.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 60 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sunset ribbon +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179)),
**Damage** 2d10+6 slashing plus 1d6 fire and 1d6 positive

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
sunset ray +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=103), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),
**Damage** 2d12+6 fire plus 1d6 positive

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +20
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Dancing Lights](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=171)
- **1st**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=159) (×3)
- **2nd**
[Faerie Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=107)
- **5th**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=148), [Searing Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=269)

**Create Golden Apple** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=134), [Transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) While the hesperid is within their bonded location, they can spin golden light around an object they're holding of up to 20 cubic feet in volume and up to 80 Bulk. Doing so condenses the object into a magic apple made of golden light with light Bulk. The golden apple reverts back to its original shape after a full day away from the hesperid's bonded location, or when the hesperid spends a single action (which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait) to end the effect.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Dual Guardians</title>

While all nymphs protect areas of natural beauty, hesperides often guard other unusual treasures of a more literal variety, sometimes including items long thought lost. Their steady temperaments and timeless loyalty ensure that they can be trusted to keep whatever they're protecting safe from harm.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-77" />
name: Hesperid
